title: EPIC G0 — The Laws of the Machine (compile-tested spikes before any interface freeze)
description:
Gate G0 precedes all construction. Its purpose: retire every load-bearing unknown of the FrankenManim program as a compile-tested spike or executed decision BEFORE workstreams W2-W11 freeze their interfaces (plan §20.1). The plan is deliberately not an MVP; sequencing is by dependency, not scope reduction, and G0 is what makes that safe.

The eight spikes: (1) object-model & buffer-lifetime, (2) renderer look study, (3) fmd-math architecture, (4) TeX corpus harvest, (5) Python extensibility, (6) cross-platform determinism, (7) dependency-closure audit + SUITE.lock, (8) accelerator (GPU annex) proof.

Why this exists: Rev 2's adversarial audit showed the three-way contradiction between exact conformance, cross-platform reproducibility, and improved mathematics. Rev 3/4 resolved it by decision (conformance-to-pixels dropped; correctness + beauty + sovereignty kept), but several architectural decisions (arena object model, fixed-point vs floating certified raster arithmetic, fmd-math atom-engine shape, GPU IR mapping) can only be ratified by running code. Discovering at W5 that the render IR maps poorly to a GPU would be expensive; discovering it at G5 after CPU assumptions hardened would be worse.

Exit criteria: all eight spike beads closed with their decisions recorded in the plan's decision log (§23, D-01..D-24 amended as needed) and open questions OQ-1/OQ-2/OQ-10 resolved or explicitly deferred with an owner. No W2-W11 interface freeze may happen while this epic is open.

title: EPIC W1 — Substrate & Contracts (fmn-core/dmath/hash/config/platform + Gauntlet bootstrap)
description:
Workstream W1 (plan §20.2): the foundation everything else stands on, and deliberately the FIRST deliverable is the harness that will judge everything after it (Gauntlet + self-golden rig bootstrapped before feature work).

Contents: fmn-core (constants, units, color pipeline, rate functions, contract knobs), the single seeded RNG (PCG64DXSM with named substreams and keyed per-frame forks, §6.5), fmn-dmath (owned deterministic elementary functions, §6.6), fmn-hash (canonical hashing + versioned serialization, §6.7), fmn-config (YAML-subset parser to the actual shipped file shapes, §6.4), fmn-platform (filesystem/process/clock/AssetFetcher capability traits + HardwareTopology introspection, §17.4), the input-closure definition (§16.7), the dependency-closure allowlist + SUITE.lock (§2.8, §3 D1), Windows functional CI (R18), the baseline profiling report with permanent stage-level phase instrumentation (§17.1), and G0 coordination.

Why first: the numeric doctrine (f64 semantic geometry / f32 record dtypes / tiered screen-space arithmetic, §6.1), the determinism contract, and the one-RNG rule are cross-cutting; retrofitting them is impossible. The Gauntlet-first rule means every later workstream lands with its fixtures.

Exit: all W1 task beads closed; scripts/check.sh green; the self-golden rig can bit-lock an artifact; SUITE.lock committed and CI builds only from it.

title: EPIC W2 — Chisel: the geometry kernel (fmn-geom)
description:
Workstream W2 (plan §7, §20.2): the geometry kernel every mobject, animation, and glyph rides on. Depends on W1.

Contents: the QuadPath shared-anchor invariant implemented formally with exact fixtures FIRST (§7.1); ONE error-bounded cubic-to-quadratic converter serving every path — API cubics, SVG import, font outlines, smoothing — retiring the Reference's crude two-quad splitter (§7.2); the true arc-length layer (adaptive Gauss-Legendre via fsci quad) with per-revision inverse-arclength LUTs so point_from_proportion and MoveAlongPath are constant-speed under the original names (§7.3); planar path booleans with the deterministic flatten-and-clip boolean CERTIFIED FIRST as escape hatch/reference/fuzz target, then the curve-aware pipeline replacing it progressively under topology-aware acceptance (§7.4); space_ops with scipy-Rotation conventions fixed as documented semantics tested at singularities (§7.5); the SVG document processor with its security posture (§7.6); adaptive quadtree isolines honoring min_depth/max_quads budget semantics (§7.7); the ear-clip triangulation utility (§7.8).

Key doctrine: correctness is layered — heuristic fast paths (quick_point_from_proportion) survive as DOCUMENTED approximations beside the true math, never silently substituting for it.

Exit: all W2 task beads closed; path-invariant + kernel fixtures green; the flatten-first boolean certified; Behavior Notes BN-03 (true arc length) drafted.

title: EPIC W3 — Marionette: the mobject engine (fmn-mobject)
description:
Workstream W3 (plan §8, §20.2): the object model of the whole system. Depends on W1, co-designed with W2, and its core is ratified by G0 spike 1 (object-model & buffer-lifetime).

Contents: the Stage arena with generational Mob handles, rooted lifetimes, and CoW snapshots (§8.1); the RecordBuffer — interleaved f32 records matching the Reference dtypes (API surface for mobject.data and zero-copy NumPy structured views) with the specified view protocol (views pin buffers; copy-on-resize; mutation marks dirty; reallocation under a live view impossible by construction) and the Rev-4 mandatory lazy revisioned SoA/AoSoA render mirrors feeding Lumen's compiled render state (§8.2); manim's copy semantics implemented to the letter — recursive submobject/ndarray copy, updaters by reference, family-internal reference remapping (§8.3); the full family/positional API (next_to, arrange, to_edge, align_to...) locked by fixtures, bounding boxes with dirty propagation, the complete uniform inventory incl. four clip planes (§8.4); the Reference's render-order model kept as semantics with ordering-trace fixtures (§8.5); insertion-ordered dt/non-dt updaters with the Reference's add_updater(call=True) double-call bug FIXED, ValueTrackers, always_redraw, and the .animate builder's real rules (§8.6); arena snapshot serialization backing SceneState/undo/replay/fixtures (§8.7).

Exit: all W3 task beads closed; the ten G0 lifetime scenarios pass as permanent tests; Behavior Notes for §8.6 corrections drafted.

title: EPIC W4 — Choreo: the animation engine (fmn-anim)
description:
Workstream W4 (plan §9, §20.2): the animation calculus. Depends on W3.

Contents: the Animation contract — constructor surface field-for-field, begin/interpolate/finish lifecycle, get_sub_alpha lag semantics, prepare_animation accepting Animation | _AnimationBuilder only (§9.1); the RationalFrameClock — i64 frame index over fps, drift-free, emitting the Reference's exact nominal sample sequence (arange(0,run_time,1/fps)+1/fps semantics: no alpha-zero progression frame, begin()'s separate zero-interpolate, upward duration rounding, final_alpha_value) (§9.2); the six-step frame order kept exactly — animation update_mobjects(dt) → interpolate(alpha) → time advance → scene updaters over post-interpolation state → capture → emit — with the Rev-4 FramePacket freeze at capture making everything downstream pipeline-legal (§9.3); the five mechanism families and the 80 animation classes as parameterizations, the align_data path under a 1000-pair structural fixture corpus, AnimationGroup/Succession/LaggedStart interval math, squish_rate_func/time_span through one normalized-alpha pipeline, and the declarative Timeline (§9.4); the segment-purity classifier enabling embarrassingly-parallel frame rendering for pure segments, conservative by construction — any unrecognized effect demotes to stateful (§9.5, R20).

Why the order is sacred: always_redraw-style scenes semantically depend on scene updaters observing post-interpolation state. This is semantics, not implementation detail.

Exit: all W4 task beads closed; the update-order corpus green; adaptive/variable frame sampling permanently refused (D-18).

title: EPIC W5 — Lumen: the renderer (fmn-render)
description:
Workstream W5 (plan §10, §17.3-17.4, §20.2): one renderer semantically, multiple execution engines. Depends on W2 + W3 and G0 spikes 2 (look study) and 8 (accelerator proof).

Contents: the compiled retained render IR — PathTable/SegmentTable/StyleTable/InstanceTable/ImageTable + per-tile command lists, independent resource revisions (topology/geometry/transform/style/order/image/camera), compiled-path caches (y-monotone splits, arclength LUTs, stroke slabs, primitive hints), glyph/shape instancing (§10.8); two-level binning (~128x128 macrotiles → engine-sized fine tiles), painter-order-safe occlusion pruning, and the retained compositor — byte-identical tile reuse working under certified, in export as well as preview (§10.8); analytic nonzero-winding fill evaluated on the curves with homogeneous-coordinate handling for perspective and a defined interior-gradient interpolation field (§10.2); true curve-distance strokes with round caps, real joins + miter limit, arclength width/color interpolation (§10.3); 3D/camera/lighting with the KEPT look — finalize_color (reflectiveness, gloss, shadow), light at (-10,10,10), the projection constants, clip planes, glow falloff, the ~1.5px AA feel (§10.4, §1.5, Appendix B); adaptive coverage AA with tile classification and fused edge-supersampling resolve (§10.4); BOTH CPU engines — certified canonical arithmetic and the fast mixed-precision/SIMD-tier engine (§6.1, §10.5, §17.3); the FramePacket-fed frame pipeline + ExecutionPlan/render teams (§17.4); textures/images (§10.6); WASM tier 1; the Metal annex backend for Studio preview (§10.7).

The parallelism contract (§10.5 a-f, D-18) binds every task in this epic.

Exit: all W5 task beads closed; look-study calibration applied; PG-2 gates measurable; engine-equivalence suite standing.

title: EPIC W6 — Typesetting: Scribe + fmd-font + fmd-math (cross-repo)
description:
Workstream W6 (plan §11, §20.2): the program's hardest and proudest deliverable — native text and TeX mathematics with NO LaTeX on any path, ever (D-03). Cross-repo: fmd-font and fmd-math land as workspace crates in franken_markdown, governed like foundation crates via UPSTREAM_LEDGER.md and SUITE.lock (R8); FrankenManim consumes them through fmn-text and fmn-tex. Depends on W1, the fmd repo, W2 (paths), and G0 spikes 3 (architecture) and 4 (corpus harvest); the Look Gallery arrives via W5.

Contents: fmd-font — the Font module factored out of fmd's text.rs and extended with the glyf outline decoder (simple + composite glyphs → quadratic contours, phantom-point-correct metrics) over bundled OFL faces: Computer Modern (the default face — the 3b1b typographic identity), IBM Plex Sans, CM Typewriter, Noto Sans Math (§11.1); fmd-math — the TeX-math layout engine in the KaTeX/Typst class: the real grammar surface, the eight atom classes + inter-atom spacing table, style propagation, Appendix-G placement parameters over hand-calibrated synthesized math metrics, extensible delimiters with drawn-path universal fallback, environments, user macros + preamble packs (§11.4); native span-map provenance killing the Reference's render-twice-and-align hack (§11.3); fmn-text shaping/markup/layout with manim's width semantics and StringMobject indexing conventions (§11.2); fmn-tex integration, content-addressed typeset caching, and the pre-play typesetting preflight (§11.5); the public coverage ratchet with precise named unsupported-construct errors (§11.5, R1 — there is deliberately no fallback to reach for).

Exit: all W6 task beads closed; tier-1 construct set lays out correctly (published-rule verification) and beautifully (Gallery); fmd itself renders \$...\$ in HTML/PDF via the same crates.

title: EPIC W7 — Menagerie + Atlas: the mobject library (fmn-library)
description:
Workstream W7 (plan §12, §20.2): the 161 non-runtime, non-animation classes of Appendix A as thin compositions over Marionette + Chisel + Scribe. Depends on W3, W4, W5; W6 for every text/math-bearing class.

Contents: the geometry lineage — Arc family under ONE clean arc-density rule replacing the Reference's three inconsistent conventions (BN-09), Line/Arrow/Vector with tip-attachment algebra placing by true arc length, polygons, rectangles, TipableVMobject — constructors locked by structural fixtures against the Reference where formulas intentionally coincide (§12.1); coordinate systems (Atlas) — CoordinateSystem protocol (c2p/p2c, graphing with discontinuity handling, Riemann rectangles, areas), Axes/ThreeDAxes/NumberPlane/ComplexPlane/NumberLine with tick/number placement locked by glyph-sequence tests on native text, ParametricCurve, FunctionGraph, ImplicitFunction over the §7.7 isolines (§12.2); the de-TeX'd natives — Brace as a parametric path generator, Matrix delimiters from fmd-math's extensible-delimiter engine, native Checkmark/Exmark, pure-text DecimalNumber/Integer — removing the deepest early typesetting dependency and yielding objects that scale properly by construction (§11.6, §12.3); 3D solids + fields — ParametricSurface UV-grid semantics, VectorField with fmn-dmath tanh normalization, StreamLines on fsci adaptive RK45 with dense output seeded from named RNG substreams, TracedPath/TracingTail tracing by true length, DotCloud/GlowDot, ImageMobject, ThreeDModel OBJ-subset (§12.4); enhanced-tier Graph mobjects (fnx, audited layout kernels + determinism rules) and Data mobjects (fp) — never blocking core gates (§12.5-12.6); the drawings whimsy shelf, ported late, one self-golden each (§12.7).

Exit: all W7 task beads closed; the census coverage recorded in the Parity Ledger.

title: EPIC W8 — Reel: frames, codecs, cache, output (fmn-frame/codec/cache/output)
description:
Workstream W8 (plan §14, §20.2): everything between rendered coverage and bytes on disk. Depends on W1; orchestration ties into W5.

Contents: fmn-frame — buffers, pixel formats (RGBA8/BGRA8, RGBA16F intermediates, NV12/P010 video planes, canonical RGBA for certified), transfer functions (§14.1); fmn-codec — PNG decode across the real ecosystem (full color-type matrix, Adam7, gamma/sRGB policy, decompression-bomb rejection) and encode, JPEG decode (baseline + progressive, restart markers, EXIF orientation, CMYK policy), GIF encode (median-cut + Floyd-Steinberg), y4m/raw, WAV in/out, SVG export (§14.2); deterministic parallel PNG-sequence encode with fixed DEFLATE block boundaries — identical bytes on any thread count, so the canonical form survives under certified (§14.2); the negotiated ffmpeg boundary v2 — FFMPEG_PROTOCOL.md: native orientation (no vflip ever) and native transfer (no obligatory eq), negotiated pixel format/primaries/range/stride/encoder, hardware encoders (VideoToolbox/NVENC) as a standard-mode knob riding the same sandboxed subprocess boundary, two-stage audio mux, --subdivide, fake-ffmpeg CI, full fingerprinting into provenance (§14.3, D-02, D-23); the ordered asynchronous emitter over preallocated frame rings — Lumen never stalls on a sink, no sink observes out-of-order or torn frames (§14.1); fmn-cache — content-addressed on fmn-hash: atomic writes, cross-process locks, versioned namespaces, corruption recovery, eviction, ceilings (§14.4); the WAV/PCM sound mixer with its fully specified matrix and deterministic mix order (§14.5).

Exit: all W8 task beads closed; a scene exports via pure-native paths with ffmpeg absent (capability errors name alternatives), and via negotiated ffmpeg when present.

title: EPIC W9 — Proscenium: scene runtime, Studio, CLI (fmn-scene/studio/cli)
description:
Workstream W9 (plan §13, §20.2): the runtime users actually live in. Depends on W4, W5, W8.

Contents: the Scene state machine on the rational clock — construct, add/remove/bring_to_front/clear, play/wait/wait_until, SceneState snapshot/restore at Reference scope, EndScene, ThreeDScene defaults, presenter mode, the windowed fps=30 override (§13.1); the event dispatcher and InteractiveScene's selection/grab/resize/color/clipboard behaviors, keyboard-map compatible (§13.2); iteration WITHOUT dlopen — supervisor + scene-worker subprocess: code change → incremental rebuild → worker restart → restore nearest serialized checkpoint → replay from the last valid barrier; crashes isolate to the worker; fmn-python preserves the literal IPython embed()/checkpoint_paste() workflow (§13.3); the replay journal + effect model — commands, effects, RNG substream states at barriers, content hashes of every file/font/asset read, opaque barriers for non-replayable operations, conservative invalidation; doubles as the §9.5 purity classifier and the pipeline's barrier vocabulary (§13.4, R16); the Studio — loopback-only with per-request capability tokens, multipart-PNG baseline stream, annex-accelerated preview on supported Apple silicon, timeline scrubbing over Timeline + checkpoints, the inspector (family tree, live record fields, uniforms, typeset span maps), debug overlays, kitty/sixel TUI (§13.5); the full CLI — the Reference's flag surface where it still means something, --reproducible, fmn batch under asupersync with budgets, fmn doctor (§13.6).

Exit: all W9 task beads closed; the Studio security model reviewed; edit-to-frame < 1 s on the PG-4 scenario.

title: EPIC W10 — Portals + Gauntlet: fmn-python, corpus, verification (fmn-conformance)
description:
Workstream W10 (plan §15-§17, §20.2): the two front doors proven, and the verification machine that judges the whole program. Spans all workstreams; live from W1.

Contents: the ONE API schema — classes, methods, parameters, defaults, CLI flags, config keys — generating Rust structs/builders, fmn-python signatures, Parity Ledger rows, and docs; drift between front doors is a build error (§16.2); fmn-python — the manimlib surface with real subclassing (MRO/override dispatch with engine callbacks), writable __dict__, custom data_dtypes, live data/uniforms/submobjects mutation under the §8.2 view protocol, copy/deepcopy/pickle, GIL/reentrancy rules, import-surface conformance vs the actual wildcard namespace (§15.2); the binding-tax program — cached method resolution, batched crossings, GIL released across native work, the opt-in acceleration ladder (batched → array → native updaters), callback-heavy scene detection with phase breakdown (§15.2, PG-8); VIDEO_CORPUS.lock + documented shims + the short Strategy-B adapter list, with the CC BY-NC-SA fixture policy (§15.3-15.4); the symbol-granular Parity Ledger with semantic status tiers (same | improved | tiered | excluded) regenerating the coverage badge (§16.1); the three test planes + engine-equivalence suite (§16.3); tolerance doctrine (§16.4); parser/typesetter fuzzing with resource budgets (§16.5); the out-of-tier ledger (§16.6); certified-determinism closure automation (§16.7); the Behavior Notes register (§16.8); the performance rig + PG-1..PG-8 + PG-A gates with pinned profiles (§17.2).

Exit: all W10 task beads closed; G4a and G4b criteria mechanically checkable.

title: EPIC W11 — Distribution (binaries, wheels, WASM, bundles, reproducible releases)
description:
Workstream W11 (plan §19, §20.2): packaging the sovereignty story. Depends on W8, W9, W10.

Contents: release binaries with per-SIMD-tier artifacts (portable / x86-64-v3 / x86-64-v4 / aarch64+NEON) and install/launch selection UX (§17.3); the install script; Python wheels — CPython x platform ABI matrix and the manimlib namespace policy (§15, §20.2); npm/WASM packaging for the tier-1 frame renderer and tier-2 serialized-timeline player (§10.7); font + license bundles (OFL texts for Computer Modern, IBM Plex Sans, CM Typewriter, Noto Sans Math) and embedded Studio UI assets; cache/config directory conventions; reproducible releases — the release pipeline itself honors the determinism doctrine; CI-measured compile-time and binary-size budgets (R19).

Why it gets its own workstream and gate: one-binary installation IS leapfrog #1 (§22) — the whole point of deleting LaTeX/Pango/fontconfig is that a bare machine renders every built-in scene identically with a single artifact. Distribution failures (missing font bundle, wrong tier selection, broken wheel ABI) would silently destroy that promise, so W11 is engineering, not an afterthought.

Exit: all W11 task beads closed; G5's distribution criteria met.

title: G0-1: Object-model & buffer-lifetime spike (Stage arena + live-NumPy-view protocol + fluent-API prototype)
description:
Compile-tested spike ratifying (or amending) the entire object-model design of §8.1-8.2 and §15.1 before Marionette's interfaces freeze (decision D-11: arena + handles PENDING G0 ratification; the fluent Rust API is whatever this prototype proves).

Scope — the ten lifetime scenarios must all compile and behave correctly against the prototype: (1) detached construction (mobject built before any scene exists), (2) cross-group composition, (3) multiple parents sharing a submobject, (4) removal from a scene with live handles outstanding, (5) the two-scene policy, (6) copy() with family-internal reference remapping, (7) Python proxy identity surviving collection round-trips, (8) live NumPy structured views surviving resize/align/become/glyph-rebuild per the §8.2 view protocol (views pin their buffer; copy-on-resize with NumPy-natural detach semantics; mutation marks render state dirty; reallocation under a live view impossible by construction), (9) updater closures capturing handles, (10) snapshot/restore (CoW).

Also in scope: the compiling fluent-API prototype for the Rust front door — current lean is a scoped `stage` context + deferred-command .animate recording, which mirrors the Reference's builder semantics anyway (§15.1).

Why this is first-priority: every crate above fmn-mobject consumes these decisions. Retrofitting a different ownership model after W3-W7 exist would be a rewrite. Risk register R12 (zero-copy buffer lifetime, High): if the view protocol proves unsound, the declared fallback is copy-based export for affected surfaces, Behavior-Noted — that decision must be made HERE, not discovered later.

Acceptance: a compiling prototype crate exercising all ten scenarios as tests; a written ratification-or-amendment note updating D-11; the view-protocol rules stated precisely enough for W3 to implement from this text alone.

title: G0-2: Renderer look study — calibrate the analytic renderer against captured Reference imagery
description:
Executed calibration study fixing Lumen's aesthetic parameters BEFORE W5 scales (§20.1 spike 2 → §10, §6.3). The product contract is "correct AND beautiful, judged as at-least-as-good" — this spike turns "the same feel, cleaner" from aspiration into a measured statement.

Scope: render the calibration set with the prototype analytic renderer — fills with per-vertex gradients, self-intersecting paths, every joint/cap combination, glow dots, 3D lighting spheres/surfaces, text samples — side-by-side against one-time captured Reference imagery (no certified Pango/llvmpipe capture environment is maintained; capture once, keep forever). Fix, by decision recorded in the plan: (a) the adaptive-AA defaults and tile-escalation thresholds (§10.4), (b) the AA profile and the ~1.5px weight, (c) the smooth "auto" join tuning and miter-limit default (§10.3), (d) gradient behavior — confirming the kept interpolate_color sqrt(lerp(c1^2,c2^2,a)) form and RMS average_color composite correctly in linear light (§6.3), (e) the finalize_color lighting match — (reflectiveness, gloss, shadow), light at (-10,10,10), dark_shift 0.2 (§1.5, Appendix B), (f) the cubic→quad converter tolerance default so curve fidelity visibly exceeds the Reference's (§7.2).

Why: the Reference's look constants are the aesthetic DNA of 3Blue1Brown and are KEPT deliberately (D-04); but they were tuned for a GPU pipeline full of workarounds (signed-alpha fills, x1.06 composite un-mangle, polyline ribbon strokes). Porting the constants onto honest mathematics shifts their visual effect; only side-by-side calibration can close that loop.

Acceptance: the calibration gallery checked in (small, public, permissively-licensed primitives); a parameters document with every chosen constant + rationale; risk R2's standing mitigation (Gallery review as gate input) stood up.

title: G0-3: fmd-math architecture spike — parse-and-layout proof over the hard constructs
description:
Compile-tested spike proving the shape of the math-layout engine BEFORE the fmd-math crate's API freezes (§20.1 spike 3 → §11.4; resolves OQ-2). This is the program's critical-path risk R1 (Critical severity: no LaTeX fallback exists, deliberately) — the spike exists to fail fast if the architecture is wrong.

Scope — parse AND lay out, with correct output paths: \frac (nested), sub/superscripts (including simultaneous sub+sup with correct kerning), radicals with indices, a \left( ... \right) delimiter pair at three sizes, a big operator with limits (\sum in display and text style), and a small matrix environment. This forces every load-bearing architectural question: (a) the atom-classification + inter-atom spacing engine shape (the eight TeX atom classes, the spacing table, style propagation display/text/script/scriptscript with \mathchoice-class behavior); (b) the math-metrics synthesis method for bundled Computer Modern — axis height, x-height, quad, sup/sub shifts, radical parameters, fraction gaps, hand-calibrated in the spirit of OpenType MATH (CM's TFM-era metrics must be reconstructed for our sfnt parsing); (c) the extensible-delimiter strategy — CM extension-glyph assembly vs drawn-path construction vs hybrid (OQ-2), with drawn paths as the universal fallback so NO requested size can fail.

Depends on fmd-font being far enough along to decode CM glyph outlines (the fmd-font factoring task) — coordinate cross-repo via the upstream ledger.

Acceptance: rendered output paths for all spike constructs, visually reviewed against LaTeX renders of the same input; a written architecture decision resolving OQ-2; the fmd-math public-API sketch frozen at spike shape until G2 (R8 pivot rule); explicit go/no-go on the Appendix-G parameter approach.

title: G0-4: Corpus harvest — the TeX-string multiset from 3b1b/videos + Reference constructors
description:
Executed data-gathering task defining fmd-math's tier-1 scope empirically (§20.1 spike 4 → §11.5). With no LaTeX fallback, coverage discipline replaces fallback discipline: tier 1 must be scoped by MEASURED occurrence, not aspiration (R1 mitigation).

Scope: extract the TeX-string multiset from (a) the pinned 3b1b/videos tree and (b) the Reference's own constructors (Tex-backed classes, preset strings) — static extraction (regex/AST over the Python sources for Tex/TexText call sites and string constants) plus a one-time instrumented run where feasible. Deduplicate, count occurrences, tokenize into constructs (commands, environments, symbol classes), and rank constructs by occurrence weight. Deliverables: (1) the ranked construct table — this IS the tier-1 definition (T1: the §11.4 surface covering the overwhelming mass of real formulas; T2: the long tail — \substack, uncommon decorations, exotic environments — scheduled by rank); (2) the ratchet denominator — the fixed corpus against which the public coverage ratchet (occurrence-weighted and unique-string, parse-vs-layout success split) is computed forever after; (3) a licensing note: corpus strings feed private fixtures under the CC BY-NC-SA fixture policy (§15.3) — the public ratchet publishes numbers and construct names, not the 3b1b-authored strings themselves.

Why it matters beyond fmd-math: the harvest also seeds VIDEO_CORPUS.lock scene selection (W10) and tells us which Tex-backed library classes are actually load-bearing.

Acceptance: the harvest tooling checked in (repeatable against the pin); the ranked table + tier-1 cut committed; the ratchet's counting rules written down precisely enough that W6 implements the dashboard from this text alone.

title: G0-5: Python extensibility spike — real subclassing against the prototype bridge
description:
Compile-tested spike proving fmn-python's hardest promise before its contract freezes (§20.1 spike 5 → §15.2; risk R10, High). The manimlib compatibility claim is SOURCE compatibility with normal Python object semantics — which means dynamic subclassing must actually work against a Rust engine, not merely wrap it.

Scope — a real Python subclass exercising, against the prototype PyO3 bridge: overriding init_data / init_points / init_uniforms / interpolate with the ENGINE calling back into the Python overrides at the right lifecycle points (MRO/override dispatch, not static binding); arbitrary instance attributes in __dict__ participating in copy remapping; a custom data_dtype declared by the subclass flowing through the RecordBuffer schema machinery; live self.data["point"] structured-view mutation under the §8.2 view protocol; identity/hashing/weakref behavior across engine collection round-trips; exception mapping across the FFI boundary both directions.

Interlocks: consumes G0-1's object-model prototype (the Python proxy identity scenario is shared). Findings feed PG-8's class-tiered binding-tax budgets — measure and record the per-callback crossing cost observed, since the §15.2 binding-tax program (cached method resolution, batched crossings, GIL release windows) is designed against these numbers.

Acceptance: the spike subclass suite green against the prototype bridge; a written gap list — anything that cannot be honored gets tiered in the Parity Ledger BEFORE G4a per R10's pivot rule; measured crossing-cost numbers recorded for the W10 perf-rig baseline.

title: G0-6: Determinism spike — one nontrivial frame hashed across four platforms; fix the certified arithmetic
description:
Executed decision spike resolving OQ-1: floating-point + fmn-dmath vs fixed-point raster arithmetic for certification (§20.1 spike 6 → §6.6, §10.5; risk R11, High).

Scope: render ONE nontrivial frame — it must exercise transcendental-dense paths (arcs, per-vertex atan2 joints, rate functions, glow falloff), gradient fills, strokes with joins, and alpha compositing — through the prototype renderer on linux-x86-64, linux-aarch64, macos-aarch64, and windows-x86-64, and compare raw-buffer hashes. Two sub-questions: (a) with f32/f64 floating screen-space arithmetic + fmn-dmath transcendentals + fixed-order reductions + FMA off, are the four platforms bit-identical? If yes, floating suffices for certified; if no, adopt the canonical fixed-point raster boundary (fixed-point screen coordinates + integer coverage accumulation at defined precision, explicit rounding) that §10.5 sketches. (b) Decide the certified matrix: linux-x86-64/linux-aarch64/macos-aarch64 certified from day one; windows-x86-64 functional-CI from W1 with bit-certification a SEPARATE declared decision (OQ-6, §16.7).

Prerequisite: an fmn-dmath prototype for the exercised functions (sin/cos/atan2/exp/tanh/sqrt at minimum) with documented accuracy — this spike is also fmn-dmath's first accuracy/portability shakedown.

Why now: everything in §10.5's parallelism contract, §6.1's tiered numeric doctrine, and the §17.3 SIMD lane rules is written against whichever arithmetic wins. Changing the answer after W5 exists would invalidate every self-golden.

Acceptance: hashes + methodology committed; OQ-1 resolved with the decision recorded in §23; the certified-matrix definition frozen into the input-closure spec (§16.7); if fixed-point wins, the precision/rounding constants specified.

title: G0-7: Dependency-closure audit + SUITE.lock — the governed universe, generated and enforced
description:
Executed audit standing up the D1 governed-closure doctrine as running enforcement (§20.1 spike 7 → §3 D1, §2.8; leapfrog #12).

Scope: (1) generate the COMPLETE transitive dependency closure of the planned workspace — the suite's own dependencies, FFI crates (PyO3), proc-macros, build scripts, platform crates — and produce the per-package allowlist: name/version/source/checksum, feature set, license, build-script and proc-macro status, native-link status, build-time network access, unsafe-audit status, reason-for-inclusion, owner, upgrade policy; runtime / FFI / platform-WASM / build / dev / fuzz dependencies tracked under separate policies. (2) Commit SUITE.lock: exact commits + checksums for every foundation repo (franken_numpy, frankenscipy, franken_markdown INCLUDING the new fmd-font/fmd-math crates, franken_networkx, frankenpandas, frankentorch, asupersync), the rustc nightly (exact version, no "or later"), and the certified target-feature sets per SIMD build tier (§17.3). (3) Implement the audit as an owned fmn-conformance check — CI fails on ANY package not in the allowlist. (4) Stand up the wasm-target audit: which closure members compile for wasm32, which need feature-gating or shims at fmn-platform (R15).

Pre-authorized beyond the suite (per D1): PyO3 (fmn-python only), clap (cli feature), wasm-bindgen (wasm feature). Nothing else without amending the allowlist deliberately.

Acceptance: SUITE.lock + allowlist committed; the CI check live and failing on an injected unlisted package (negative test); the wasm audit report written with tier assignments; upgrade ritual documented (deliberate, Gauntlet-diffed — R17).

title: G0-8: Accelerator proof spike — stroke SDF + AA resolve as frankentorch Metal kernels into a Studio-preview frame
description:
Compile-tested spike proving the render IR maps to a GPU BEFORE W5 freezes it (§20.1 spike 8 → §10.7, §17.5; resolves OQ-10's opening; risk R21 context). Rev 4 moved this from W5-time to G0 deliberately: discovering IR/GPU mismatch late would be expensive, and discovering it after CPU-specific assumptions harden would be worse.

Scope: express the render IR's highest-ROI stage — stroke signed-distance evaluation + AA resolve (the dominant cost of typical 2D scenes, per-pixel independent) — as frankentorch kernels on Metal, running end-to-end into an actual Studio-preview frame on Apple silicon: IR upload through ft shared buffers (unified memory makes the handoff nearly free), kernel dispatch, composite, present. Simultaneously open the CUDA-via-ft feasibility question as an upstream-ledger item (§2.9) — the annex's second backend is spiked on the ledger before any production claim.

Hard constraints that survive regardless of outcome: the annex is standard-mode ONLY, never certified (D-18's permanent refusal of GPU in the certified path); excluded from core gates; measured under its own PG-A profiles; backend identity journaled into the input closure; frankentorch is the ONLY GPU gateway — no wgpu ever (a dependency of that size is a D1 audit catastrophe and duplicates ft).

Acceptance: a Metal-rendered preview frame demonstrably produced from the prototype IR; a written mapping report — which IR tables/stages translate cleanly, which need layout changes (feed those changes back into the §10.8 IR design NOW); the CUDA ledger item filed with its feasibility notes; go/defer recorded for G3's annex-preview requirement (G3 has a declared CPU fallback if the annex misses — OQ-12).

title: W1: Workspace scaffolding — cargo workspace, crate skeletons, pinned toolchain, scripts/check.sh, CI bootstrap
description:
Stand up the franken_manim cargo workspace exactly per the §19 crate map: fmn-core, fmn-dmath, fmn-hash, fmn-config, fmn-platform, fmn-frame, fmn-codec, fmn-cache, fmn-geom, fmn-mobject, fmn-anim, fmn-render, fmn-text, fmn-tex, fmn-library, fmn-scene, fmn-studio, fmn-output, fmn-cli, fmn-conformance, fmn-runtime, fmn-python — as skeleton crates with their dependency edges declared and pointing STRICTLY downward (cycle-free by construction; add a CI check that the crate graph matches the plan's DAG).

Details that matter: (a) rust-toolchain.toml pinning the EXACT nightly (no "or later") — the same one SUITE.lock will record; (b) #![forbid(unsafe_code)] at every crate root EXCEPT fmn-python (the one PyO3 crate, D3); (c) workspace lints: clippy -D warnings baseline; (d) feature axes wasm, accel-annex (metal/cuda sub-features), batch, cli — all default-OFF; (e) scripts/check.sh running cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test in order, stopping on first failure (AGENTS.md's mandatory gate; CI wires this script rather than duplicating commands); (f) GitHub Actions CI bootstrap on linux-x86-64 with the pinned toolchain.

Why now: every other bead lands code in this skeleton. The downward-only dependency rule and the forbid(unsafe) posture are structural properties — cheap to enforce from commit one, expensive to retrofit.

Acceptance: cargo check green across the empty workspace; scripts/check.sh green; CI runs it; crate-DAG check live; a deliberate compile failure demonstrates -D warnings is enforced.

title: W1: fmn-core — constants, units, color pipeline, rate functions (the shared language of every manim scene)
description:
Implement fmn-core's semantic bedrock (§6.2, §6.3, §6.8).

Constants & units (§6.2): FRAME_HEIGHT = 8 and the coordinate conventions, direction constants (UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT/OUT/IN...), buffs, DEG/TAU, default stroke widths, the manim color palette, STROKE_WIDTH_CONVERSION = 0.01, the resolution table, the 30 fps default. These are kept EXACTLY — they are the shared language of every manim scene — and locked by a constants test against the Reference module (generate the fixture once from the pinned Reference source).

Color (§6.3): one pipeline, ours: colors decode to linear light, composite premultiplied, encode at the output transfer function. Manim's gradient aesthetic is deliberately preserved: interpolate_color keeps the Reference's sqrt(lerp(c1^2, c2^2, alpha)) form and average_color its RMS form, applied within the defined model. Full named palette, color_gradient, owned colormap tables (displacing matplotlib's), the hwb()/color-mix() machinery reused from fmd, and an Oklab interpolation OPTION. Draft Behavior Note BN-04 ("correct compositing, familiar gradients").

Rate functions (§6.8): the complete catalog with the same formulas (smooth, rush_into, there_and_back, wiggle, exponential_decay, ...), locked at 10^4 samples against the Reference; squish_rate_func and not_quite_there marked as combinators in the API schema.

Numeric doctrine hooks (§6.1): establish the f64-semantic / f32-record type aliases and the NaN/-0 canonicalization helpers used at serialization boundaries.

Acceptance: constants/rate-function parity tables green; color round-trip oracle tests (decode-encode identity, premultiply laws) green; BN-04 drafted.

title: W1: The single RNG — PCG64DXSM with named substreams and keyed per-frame forks
description:
Implement fmn-core's one-RNG rule (§6.5, D-06). The Reference carries TWO seeded legacy streams (CPython random + NumPy legacy RandomState); with output parity dropped, both are irrelevant — FrankenManim standardizes on fnp-random's PCG64DXSM, already bit-exact against NumPy for explicit seeds.

Requirements: (a) a single seeded root stream exposed via fmn-core; OS entropy when unseeded; (b) NAMED SUBSTREAMS derived per subsystem (layout jitter, stream-line seeding, sampling, shuffle...) so features don't perturb each other's sequences — adding a new consumer must never shift an existing consumer's draws; (c) snapshot/restore of full stream state into SceneState and the replay journal; (d) KEYED PER-FRAME FORKS (Rev 4, load-bearing for §9.5): a frame's stream state derives from (substream id, frame index) — NEVER from sequential pulls consumed in scheduler completion order. This is the property that makes frame-parallel rendering replay-identical BY CONSTRUCTION, and the reason per-thread completion-order RNG is on §10.5's permanent refusal list (D-18); (e) render-affecting map iteration uses ordered maps only — audit helper + lint note.

Testing: substream independence tests (adding draws on stream A leaves stream B's sequence untouched); fork determinism tests (fork(sub, k) identical regardless of call order/thread); snapshot/restore round-trip; NumPy seed-parity vectors via fnp.

Draft Behavior Note BN-01: seeded scenes reproduce within FrankenManim, not across engines.

Acceptance: all above tests green; the fork API documented for Choreo (§9.5) and StreamLines (§12.4) consumers.

title: W1: fmn-dmath — owned deterministic elementary functions (the certified transcendental layer)
description:
Implement fmn-dmath (§6.6, D-17): an owned, fixed-implementation elementary-function layer, because std's transcendentals defer to platform libm and differ across glibc/macOS/WASM — and this engine is transcendental-dense (arc construction, per-vertex atan2 joints, rate functions, glow falloffs, tanh normalization, Euler conversions).

Scope: sin, cos, tan, atan, atan2, asin, acos, exp, ln, log2, pow, sqrt, cbrt, tanh, sinh, cosh at minimum — f64 and f32 entry points; fixed polynomial/range-reduction implementations with DOCUMENTED accuracy bounds (state max ULP error per function per domain); bit-identical on every certified target (linux-x86-64, linux-aarch64, macos-aarch64, windows-x86-64 functional) — locked by cross-platform vector files in CI: a committed table of (input, expected-bits) pairs covering normals, denormals, near-branch-cut and near-singularity inputs.

Mode policy: standard mode MAY use fast paths (std/libm); certified uses fmn-dmath EXCLUSIVELY. The seam is a trait or function-table chosen at engine construction, never a scattered cfg.

SIMD posture (§6.6, §17.3): design the kernels as fixed-coefficient, fixed-order polynomials so they vectorize bit-reproducibly later — same operation order in scalar and every lane width. Do NOT vectorize in this bead; just don't preclude it (no table-lookup designs that defeat lanes).

Interlock: G0-6 (determinism spike) consumes a prototype of this crate; its cross-platform findings may force implementation adjustments — budget for one revision pass after the spike lands.

Acceptance: accuracy docs per function; cross-platform vector CI green; property tests (range reduction identities, symmetry laws); the standard/certified seam demonstrated in a test.

title: W1: fmn-hash — canonical hashing + versioned canonical serialization
description:
Implement fmn-hash (§6.7, D-17): the content-addressing and serialization substrate shared by the cache, snapshots, provenance manifests, asset/font hashes, and repro bundles.

Scope: (a) an owned SHA-256-class primitive (built in-house per the governed closure — no external crypto crates) with incremental hashing, hex/display forms, and test vectors from the standard; (b) versioned canonical serialization: magic numbers, explicit endianness, schema ids + versions, checksums, DEFINED field order (never map-iteration order), size limits, unknown-field policy, and a migration policy — durable formats are versioned additive-minor/breaking-major FROM DAY ONE (the one place backwards compatibility is a real obligation, per AGENTS.md); (c) NaN/-0 canonicalization enforced at every serialization boundary (coordinate with fmn-core's helpers).

Why owned and why canonical: certified determinism (§16.7) hashes the complete input closure — sources, config bytes, seeds, font files, engine identities — and equality of hashes must mean equality of meaning. Any nondeterminism in serialization (map order, platform float text formatting, locale) silently breaks certification, which is why this layer is specified rather than derived.

Consumers to design for now: fmn-cache keys (typeset results: string + pack + font hashes), arena snapshots (§8.7), SceneState, the replay journal's content hashes (§13.4), the provenance sidecar manifest (§16.7), .npy interop via fnp-io for per-field fixture data.

Acceptance: hash test vectors green; serialization round-trip + golden-bytes tests (a committed byte-exact fixture per schema version); a negative test proving unknown-field and size-limit policies fire; fuzz target for the deserializer registered for W10's fuzzing bead.

title: W1: fmn-config — YAML-subset parser to the actual shipped file shapes + precedence + preamble-pack registry
description:
Implement fmn-config (§6.4). The Reference ships two YAML configs; users have custom_config.yml files. We parse the ACTUAL shipped file shapes exactly — not YAML-the-standard: plain/quoted/block scalars with chomping indicators, tuple-string values, comments, duplicate-key policy (define it: last-wins with a warning), UTF-8, depth/size limits — with precise diagnostics (line/column, expected-vs-found) for everything else. This is an owned parser under the governed closure (no yaml crates), and a fuzz target from day one (§16.5: parser budgets — nesting depth, size).

Precedence: the Reference's rules exactly — built-in defaults → user config file → CLI flags — with the merged, typed config exposed to every subsystem via one struct family generated from the API schema (§16.2) once that exists; hand-write the structs now, migrate to codegen when W10's schema bead lands.

The preamble-pack registry: the Reference's tex_templates.yml concept is reborn as fmd-math preamble packs — named macro/symbol bundles (§11.4) — with a compatibility mapping for the common templates. This bead owns the registry surface (naming, lookup, config keys); the packs' CONTENT is W6's business.

Also here: the determinism-mode and engine/backend config keys (standard | certified; cpu | metal | cuda; aa policy; thread policy) as typed knobs with the constraint that quality/backend knobs may never change semantics (§4) — enforce by construction (they select engines/policies, never feed scene code).

Acceptance: fixture files exercising every supported scalar/shape; diagnostics golden tests; precedence matrix test; fuzz target registered; the pack-registry API documented for W6.

title: W1: fmn-platform — capability traits (fs/process/clock/AssetFetcher) + HardwareTopology introspection
description:
Implement fmn-platform (§6, §17.4). Two halves:

(1) Capability traits: filesystem, process spawning (the ffmpeg boundary's substrate — argv-only, env allowlist, timeouts, process-tree cancellation live HERE as the one sanctioned subprocess mechanism), monotonic + wall clocks, and the host-provided AssetFetcher trait — the ONLY place network exists; no TLS in core (D2). Every capability is a trait object the engine receives, never an ambient global: this is what makes the deterministic lab, WASM tiers, and the Studio's isolated worker possible. Provide std implementations + test doubles (virtual fs, fake clock, scripted AssetFetcher).

(2) HardwareTopology introspection (Rev 4, §17.4): physical/logical cores, performance classes (P/E), cache groups and NUMA nodes/CCDs, SIMD tier detection (portable/v3/v4/NEON), annex device presence, available memory, encoder capabilities — processor-group-aware on Windows (systems above 64 logical processors span groups and explicit scheduling code must know it). This feeds fmn-runtime's ExecutionPlan derivation (W5 bead): engine, frames in flight, render-team count/threads, tile dimensions, SIMD tier, scratch sizes, output pixel format.

Platform notes to honor from §17.6: no core pinning on Apple silicon (no clean no-unsafe API; output is thread-count-independent so placement cannot affect bits anyway); QoS class mapping is a consumer concern but the topology must expose what it needs.

Acceptance: trait suite + test doubles; topology snapshots from at least linux-x86-64 and one aarch64 committed as fixtures; Windows processor-group handling unit-tested against synthetic topologies; doc comments making the capability doctrine explicit for every later crate.

title: W1: Gauntlet bootstrap — self-golden rig, Reference imagery capture, fixture interchange (FIRST deliverable)
description:
Bootstrap fmn-conformance BEFORE feature work (§20.2 W1: "Gauntlet + self-golden rig + Reference imagery capture bootstrapped first"; the plan's closing line: "the first deliverable of W1 remains the harness that will judge everything after it").

Scope: (a) the SELF-GOLDEN rig — bit-locked artifacts (geometry snapshots at lifecycle points; frame hashes once Lumen exists) with per-platform lock files, an UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 bless flow writing .actual sidecars on mismatch (never auto-committing), and CI treating any drift as a merge blocker (D-16: self-goldens are the regression gate; Reference imagery is reference); (b) REFERENCE IMAGERY CAPTURE — the one-time capture of Reference renders for the Look Gallery and G0-2's calibration study: pin the environment used, record it, and never maintain it again (no certified Pango/llvmpipe environment — capture once, keep forever); store captures under the CC BY-NC-SA fixture policy (private gallery fixtures; public permissive primitive corpus; per-scene attribution, §15.3); (c) FIXTURE INTERCHANGE — .npy via fnp-io as the cross-engine fixture format (constructor point arrays, family shapes from the Reference), plus the harvest/fixture-generation scripts that extract structural fixtures from the pinned Reference; (d) the tolerance doctrine encoded as reusable assertion helpers (§16.4): bit-equality for self-goldens, ULP-scaled for owned f64 math, loose f32 for cross-engine structural fixtures, explicit NaN/-0 handling.

Depends on fmn-hash (bit-locks are content hashes).

Acceptance: rig demonstrated end-to-end on a trivial artifact (create → lock → mutate → CI-fail → bless); Reference capture inventory committed with environment provenance; fixture extraction scripts runnable against the pin; tolerance helpers documented.

title: W1: Baseline profiling report + permanent stage-level phase instrumentation
description:
Publish the checked-in baseline report BEFORE performance gates are finalized (§17.1): corpus, profiler, hardware, resolution, cache state, ffmpeg in/out, phase breakdown, raw data. The premise "Python churn dominates manim render time" is MEASURED here, not assumed — gate thresholds (PG-1's 0.5x/0.35x ratios) are calibrated against these numbers.

Two deliverables: (1) THE BASELINE — profile the Python Reference on the pinned benchmark definition (hardware including its GPU, environment, 30fps config stated, cache state, ffmpeg in/out) across representative scene classes (OpeningManimExample-class, text-heavy, 3D, dense-stroke); commit the report + raw data. (2) THE PERMANENT INSTRUMENTATION — stage-level phase timing built into the engine from the start, a fixture of every profiled run forever: scene/update, Python callback, geometry compilation, render-IR synchronization, binning, raster, color conversion, annex upload/readback, ffmpeg feed, encode — timed separately, exported in the owned structured-tracing format (§18: scene → play → frame → phase → tile, JSON + flame summaries). Include the §17.2 counters as they become meaningful: reuse and cache-hit rates, dirty-tile percentage, allocations per frame, team utilization.

Why permanent: the optimization doctrine is instrument → eliminate → pipeline → vectorize → offload (§17.3); "optimization begins by deleting work, and work that isn't measured can't be deleted." PG-6's zero-steady-state-allocations gate needs the allocation counter; PG-8's binding-tax classes need the Python-callback phase.

Acceptance: baseline report committed; the tracing crate/module usable by every subsystem with near-zero overhead when disabled; a smoke test proving phase timings appear in a profiled run.

title: W1: Windows functional CI + certified-matrix CI skeleton
description:
Stand up windows-x86-64 functional CI from W1 (§20.2 W1 row; R18) and the CI skeleton for the certified matrix.

Scope: (a) Windows CI running scripts/check.sh equivalents (fmt/check/clippy/test) on the pinned nightly — FUNCTIONAL coverage: the code compiles, tests pass, behavior is correct; bit-certification for Windows is a SEPARATE declared decision (OQ-6) taken after G0-6's data, not assumed; (b) linux-aarch64 and macos-aarch64 runners wired so the certified matrix (per G0-6's outcome) has CI legs before W5 needs them — cross-platform vector checks for fmn-dmath run here from day one; (c) the thread-count determinism harness skeleton (PG-5): the {1,4,16}-thread per-commit loop and the {32,96}+ weekly cadence job, initially running whatever deterministic artifacts exist (fmn-hash goldens, later frames); (d) CI time budgets recorded (R22: per-commit on the two pinned profiles, full matrix weekly — don't let the matrix bloat CI).

Windows specifics to watch (from §17.4/§17.6): processor groups above 64 logical CPUs (fmn-platform bead handles the code; CI should include at least a synthetic-topology test), path/filesystem semantics in fmn-platform's std implementations, and locale pinning in subprocess tests.

Acceptance: all four platforms green on the skeleton workspace; the weekly job exists and runs; a doc note stating exactly what Windows is and is not promised (functional now, bit-cert decision deferred to OQ-6).

title: W2: QuadPath — the shared-anchor path model, formalized with exact fixtures first
description:
Implement Chisel's path model (§7.1, §1.2). VMobject paths are joined quadratic Béziers over a SHARED-ANCHOR layout: points = [a0, h0, a1, h1, a2, ...], length odd when nonempty, curve i = points[2i..2i+3], subpath break = a null curve whose anchor equals its handle. This invariant is API SURFACE — curve counts, partial reveals, alignment, and user code indexing points all depend on it — so it is implemented formally, with exact fixtures for empty / one-point / one-curve / closed / multi-subpath paths BEFORE any higher-level geometry lands (the fixture-first ordering is the W2 exit rule).

Also here: consider_points_equal tolerance and VectorizedPoint degeneracies preserved as API behavior; anchor modes (jagged / approx_smooth / true_smooth) and the smoothing solvers — banded solve for open paths, dense for closed (via fsci-linalg); arc primitives under ONE arc-density convention (the Reference has three inconsistent ones; ours is the unified rule, Behavior Note BN-09 — coordinate with the W7 geometry bead which consumes it); subdivision; per-vertex joint angles.

Design notes: object-space construction and the geometry APIs compute in f64 (§6.1); the QuadPath type is the shared vocabulary of Chisel, Marionette's point data, Scribe's glyph outlines, and Lumen's compiled paths — get the API right here and everything downstream composes.

Acceptance: invariant fixtures green (including adversarial: zero-length curves, coincident anchors, single-point subpaths); smoothing-solver tests against known closed forms; the arc-density rule documented with BN-09 drafted.

title: W2: One error-bounded cubic→quadratic converter for every path
description:
Implement the single cubic-reduction path (§7.2, ↷ replacing the Reference's crude two-quad splitter and its fontTools cu2qu dependency). ONE error-bounded converter (cu2qu-class: monotone subdivision to a stated tolerance) serves EVERY cubic source: API cubics (CubicBezier, SVG path data), SVG import, font outlines (TrueType glyfs are already quadratic — zero-loss passthrough), and smoothing output.

Requirements: (a) a proven error bound — the max deviation between input cubic and output quad chain is computed, not estimated, and respected to the configured tolerance; (b) determinism — same cubic + tolerance ⇒ identical output everywhere (fixed subdivision rule, no iteration-count dependence on FP quirks; f64 throughout per §6.1); (c) the tolerance DEFAULT is chosen in G0-2's look study so curve fidelity visibly exceeds the Reference's (that spike feeds a constant back into this bead — leave it configurable, land the default when G0-2 closes); (d) degenerate handling: inflection at parameter bounds, colinear control points, zero-length cubics — each with a defined, tested output.

Why one converter matters: the Reference uses a crude 2-quad splitter in the kernel and cu2qu in smooth mode — two different approximations of the same operation, drifting from each other. A single audited converter makes curve fidelity a property of the system, not of the call site.

Acceptance: error-bound property tests (random cubics: measured deviation ≤ tolerance); golden outputs for a fixture set; degenerate-case tests; a micro-benchmark (this sits on the SVG/font ingestion path).

title: W2: True arc length under the original names — adaptive quadrature + inverse-arclength LUTs
description:
Implement the proportion/length layer correctly (§7.3, BN-03). The Reference's layer is chord-distance heuristics — three mutually inconsistent approximations, with quick_point_from_proportion assuming equal curve lengths. We keep the NAMES and fix the MATH (D-05).

Scope: (a) get_arc_length returns actual arc length via adaptive Gauss–Legendre quadrature (fsci quad) with a stated tolerance; (b) point_from_proportion and MoveAlongPath consume a per-revision inverse-arclength LUT — built lazily per path revision (coordinate with §10.8's compiled-path caches: the LUT is one of the retained per-path artifacts, invalidated by geometry revision only, NOT by transform/style changes), giving constant-speed motion, as every user always assumed it worked; (c) quick_point_from_proportion REMAINS as the documented fast approximation — heuristics survive beside true math as labeled options, never silent substitutes; (d) downstream consumers get length-true placement: dashes, arrow tips, and tracers place by true length (W7 consumes this).

Behavior Note BN-03: paths animate at slightly different (correct) pacing than the Python engine; document with a migration example (a MoveAlongPath whose apparent speed changes).

Acceptance: arc-length oracle tests against closed forms (circles, parabolic arcs, lines); LUT inverse-error property tests (point_from_proportion(L(t)/total) ≈ t within tolerance); LUT-revision invalidation tests (transform does NOT rebuild; geometry edit does); constant-speed metamorphic test (equal proportion steps ⇒ equal arc steps).

title: W2: Planar path booleans — flatten-and-clip certified first, curve-aware pipeline behind topology-aware acceptance
description:
Implement path booleans (§7.4; displaces skia-pathops) in the plan's two-stage posture:

STAGE 1 (certified at W2, permanent): the deterministic FLATTEN-AND-CLIP boolean — flatten curves to tolerance, run a robust polygon clipper, return the result. Ships first as (a) the escape hatch for unsupported overlap classes, (b) the reference implementation for differential testing, (c) the primary fuzz target. Deterministic tie-breaks throughout.

STAGE 2 (progressive replacement): the curve-aware pipeline — fat-line-pruned quad–quad intersection with Newton polish, winding-number classification, and curve stitching — replacing the fallback class-by-class. Numerical posture: double-double predicates where needed, WITHOUT exactness overclaims; a WRITTEN topology spec per degenerate class (tangencies, shared endpoints, overlapping segments, zero-area lobes) before code handles it.

ACCEPTANCE IS TOPOLOGY-AWARE, not point-wise: point-in-fill sampling grids, winding-number equivalence, component/hole counts, Euler characteristic, boolean algebraic identities (A∪B = B∪A, A∖B ∩ B = ∅, ...), and multi-scale raster equivalence between stage-1 and stage-2 results; plus captured skia-pathops fixtures (generated once from the Reference toolchain) and adversarial fuzzing with resource budgets (pathological intersection counts are a DoS surface — §16.5).

Risk R4 rule: unsupported overlap classes route PERMANENTLY to the fallback rather than shipping wrong topology. Union/Difference/Intersection/Exclusion mobjects (W7) consume this.

Acceptance: stage 1 green under the full acceptance battery + fuzzing; stage-2 classes each land with their topology spec + differential tests vs stage 1.

title: W2: space_ops + Rotation conventions — scipy semantics fixed, tested at singularities
description:
Implement space_ops signature-for-signature and the quaternion/Euler conventions module (§7.5, §2.2). The Reference's space_ops follows scipy Rotation's conventions — quaternion sign, composition order, as_euler("zxz") extraction, gimbal behavior — and users' camera code assumes them. We fix these conventions AS DOCUMENTED SEMANTICS (they are meaning, not implementation detail), implemented over fnp-linalg / fsci, tested explicitly at the hard points: gimbal-lock configurations, quaternion double-cover sign choices, near-identity and near-pi rotations, axis-angle degeneracies.

Scope: rotation_matrix, quaternions (mult, conjugate, from/to axis-angle), Euler conversions with the "zxz" behavior pinned, angle_between_vectors, normalize, cross/dot conveniences, z_to_vector, rotation_about_z fast paths, get_unit_normal (Appendix B: kept), 2D↔3D point promotion rules, complex↔R2 helpers — the full call-site inventory from the Reference's space_ops module, verified against Appendix A consumers.

Determinism note: everything here is f64 semantic math (§6.1); transcendentals route through the fmn-dmath seam so certified renders get bit-stable Euler/axis conversions (CameraFrame animations are transcendental-dense).

Acceptance: convention fixtures generated from scipy on the pinned Reference environment (loose f32 tolerance per §16.4); singularity tests with DEFINED outputs (no NaN escapes); property tests (composition associativity within tolerance, double-cover q ≡ -q, round-trip Euler→quat→Euler within branch conventions); doc page stating the conventions normatively.

title: W2: The SVG document processor — user SVGs, hardened, with explicit accept/reject
description:
Implement SVGMobject's substrate as a real document processor (§7.6; displaces svgelements). Scope is USER SVGs — with dvisvgm gone, there are no TeX-output quirks to replicate; this is a clean, hardened importer.

Feature surface: XML tokenization with namespaces and entity handling; viewBox / preserveAspectRatio / unit resolution; nested transforms; defs/use with expansion limits and cycle detection; presentation-attribute and style-attribute cascade (the subset SVGs in the wild actually use — document what's out); group opacity; fill-rule (nonzero/evenodd); stroke cap/join/dash; path data parsing (all commands, arcs converted via the endpoint→center parameterization then to quads through W2's converter); basic shapes (rect/circle/ellipse/line/polyline/polygon); EXPLICIT accept-or-reject decisions for clips, masks, gradients, patterns — each either implemented, or rejected with a precise named error (never silently dropped).

Security posture (§16.5, R14 — this parses untrusted input): no external entities, no remote references of any kind, bounded document size / nesting depth / path-command counts, finite-number validation on every coordinate, decompression limits if compressed input is ever accepted. Fuzz target from day one with resource-budget assertions (must error, never hang).

Acceptance: a fixture corpus of real-world SVGs with golden path/style output; the accept/reject matrix documented; adversarial fixtures (entity bombs, deep nesting, huge counts) rejected within budget; fuzzer wired into W10's campaign.

title: W2: Scalar-field isolines + ear-clip triangulation utilities
description:
Two owned geometry utilities (§7.7–7.8):

(1) ISOLINES: an adaptive quadtree isoline extractor for ImplicitFunction (displaces the isosurfaces package), honoring the Reference's min_depth / max_quads API knobs as BUDGET semantics (the knobs bound work; they do not promise a particular mesh). Everything underdefined in the original is DEFINED here: traversal order (deterministic), truncation behavior when max_quads is hit (which regions coarsen, in what order), segment orientation convention, domain-boundary handling, and NaN/undefined-region behavior (functions may divide by zero — cells containing non-finite samples are handled by a stated rule, not UB). Quality target: "ours, and good" — adaptive refinement should visibly beat uniform sampling on curvature-varying implicit curves.

(2) EAR-CLIP: a polygon triangulator with hole support (displaces mapbox-earcut) — deterministic tie-breaks, honest acceptance criteria (valid triangulation of valid input; no Delaunay quality pretensions). It is a UTILITY for booleans' flatten fallback, mesh export, and future mesh work; the live fill path needs no triangulation at all (§10.2 rasterizes winding coverage analytically — that independence is a design feature, keep it).

Acceptance: isoline determinism tests (same field + budget ⇒ identical segments); budget-semantics tests (max_quads respected exactly); NaN-region tests; known-curve accuracy tests (circle/lemniscate implicit forms vs analytic); triangulator validity property tests (area preservation, no flipped/degenerate triangles, hole containment) over fuzzed polygons.

title: W3: The Stage arena — generational handles, rooted lifetimes, CoW snapshots (G0-1 ratified)
description:
Implement Marionette's ownership core exactly as ratified by G0-1 (§8.1, D-11). The design under ratification: a Stage arena owning all mobject storage; generational Mob handles (index + generation, invalidating on free); rooted lifetime semantics (what keeps an object alive: scene membership, parent links, explicit roots held by user code); copy-on-write snapshots backing SceneState, animation begin-states (§9.5's frame-parallel purity depends on cheap begin-state snapshots), Studio undo, and replay barriers.

Required behaviors (the ten G0-1 scenarios become this bead's permanent regression suite): detached construction; cross-group composition; multiple parents with cached family flattening (submobjects / parents / family with dirty invalidation — §1.1 API surface); removal with live handles outstanding (handles observe a defined state, never UB); the two-scene policy (defined: an object's relationship to multiple scenes is whatever G0-1 ratified); copy; Python proxy identity across collection; updater closures holding handles; snapshot/restore.

The public fluent Rust API is what the G0-1 prototype PROVED — implement that shape, not a new invention (current lean: scoped stage context + deferred-command .animate recording, §15.1).

Interlocks: the RecordBuffer bead layers per-object data storage into this arena; the family/positional bead consumes the family cache; fmn-python's proxy layer (W10) maps PyObjects onto handles.

Acceptance: the ten-scenario suite green as permanent tests; CoW snapshot cost measured (O(touched), not O(scene)); generational-safety tests (stale handle access is a defined error); miri-clean under the workspace's no-unsafe posture (trivially, but assert it).

title: W3: The RecordBuffer — interleaved f32 records, the live-view protocol, and lazy revisioned render mirrors
description:
Implement Marionette's data plane (§8.2, §1.1, D-20). Three layers, one bead, because their contracts interlock:

(1) THE AUTHORITATIVE BUFFER: interleaved f32 records with typed field views, matching the Reference dtypes exactly — [(point f32x3), (rgba f32x4)] for Mobject; the VMobject layout with stroke_rgba/stroke_width/joint_angle/fill_rgba/base_normal/fill_border_width; Surface/DotCloud/Image declaring their own; USER-DECLARED custom dtypes through the schema machinery. This layout is API surface: mobject.data is a zero-copy NumPy structured export (fnp-dtype is a structured-record engine — this is its native representation). Port field-for-field: aligned_data_keys / pointlike_data_keys, data locking as copy-elision, resize-with-interpolation, null-padding family alignment.

(2) THE VIEW PROTOCOL (G0-1-ratified): exported views pin their buffer; resize is copy-on-resize (old views detach with NumPy-natural semantics); mutation through a view marks render state dirty; reallocation under a live view is impossible by construction. While a WRITABLE live view exists, the affected object (or field, where the view is field-scoped) is conservatively invalidated each frame — a live view never silently receives weaker semantics to buy speed. Ranged editing APIs (edit_points(range, ...)-class) exist as the precise-dirty opt-in.

(3) THE RENDER MIRRORS (Rev 4, mandatory): lazy, revisioned struct-of-arrays (or AoSoA sized to the SIMD tier) mirrors of animated fields, materialized at most once per frame — the hot loops (field lerp, transforms, Lumen's IR sync) read mirrors, never the interleaved buffer; synchronization back through the view protocol happens only when a live view or API read demands it.

Acceptance: zero-copy NumPy round-trip tests (via fnp); view-protocol scenario tests (resize under live view, field-scoped dirty, ranged edits); mirror coherence property tests (mirror ≡ buffer at every observation point); mirror laziness tests (untouched fields never materialize); custom-dtype end-to-end test.

title: W3: Copy semantics — manim's rules, to the letter
description:
Implement Mobject.copy() to the Reference's exact contract (§8.3) — user code depends on these rules, so they are API semantics, not pixel trivia: recursive copy of submobjects and ndarray attributes; updater CALLABLES kept by reference (not cloned); family-internal attribute references REMAPPED (if self.arrow is also self.submobjects[2], the copy's attribute points at the copy's submobject, not the original's); selected links cleared (parents; scene backrefs) per the Reference's rules.

Also here: the interaction with the arena (copying allocates fresh handles; generation bookkeeping), with the RecordBuffer (deep field copy; live views on the ORIGINAL unaffected; the copy starts with no views), and with target/saved-state machinery (generate_target, save_state / Restore use copies — get the reference topology right or Restore breaks).

The binding tier (fmn-python, W10) layers Python-level copy.copy / copy.deepcopy / pickle onto this engine contract — design the engine-side hooks now (a stable copy-map from old handle → new handle that the Python layer can walk for __dict__ remapping).

Acceptance: fixture tests generated from the Reference's copy behavior on representative object graphs (shared submobjects, attribute aliases, updaters, nested groups); remap-correctness property test (no copy ever references an original's family member); Restore round-trip test; documented rules for which attribute categories copy deep / by-reference / cleared.

title: W3: Family tree, positional API, bounding boxes, uniforms
description:
Implement the structural/positional surface (§8.4, §1.1): the full positional API — next_to, arrange, arrange_in_grid, to_edge, to_corner, align_to, shift, move_to, center, scale, stretch, set_width/height/depth, match_* — locked by structural fixtures against the Reference (loose f32 tolerances per §16.4, since we compute in f64); bounding boxes with dirty-flag propagation through the family tree (invalidate on point mutation via the RecordBuffer dirty channel, on submobject add/remove, on transform); the cached family flattening with correct invalidation.

Uniforms (§1.1, §8.4): the COMPLETE per-object uniform inventory as typed state — including the four clip-plane slots, stroke_behind, depth-test flag, is_fixed_in_frame (a float mix, per the kept camera model), per-kind AA widths, shading triple (reflectiveness, gloss, shadow) — because scene code reads and writes mobject.uniforms directly (API surface), and Lumen's StyleTable synchronizes from it.

Design care: positional operations are defined against bounding boxes in the Reference; ours must match its box conventions (about_point/about_edge semantics, buff application, stretch vs scale) exactly enough that fixture arrays coincide. Where the Reference's box is subtly wrong (stale caches), ours is correct — any user-visible divergence gets a Behavior Note.

Acceptance: positional fixture corpus green (generated from the pinned Reference across representative shapes/nesting); bbox invalidation property tests (mutation through any channel dirties ancestors exactly once); uniform read/write round-trip via the future Python bridge surface (engine-side test now).

title: W3: The render-order model — z_index, batching partitions, ordering-trace fixtures
description:
Implement the Reference's ACTUAL render-order semantics (§8.5, §1.5): top-level sort by z_index with STABLE scene order for ties; adjacent compatible-item batching; depth-test and fixed-in-frame partitions — kept as semantics, because what draws over what is meaning, not pixels. Lumen consumes this as its draw-order model (§10.1); painter's order for transparent content descends from it (§10.4, §10.8's exact-painter-order command runs).

Deliverable is a specification-with-tests, engine-side: given a scene graph (with shared submobjects, multiple parents, mixed z_index, stroke_behind, depth-tested 3D content, fixed-in-frame overlays), produce the deterministic draw sequence. ORDERING-TRACE FIXTURES pin the tricky cases: a submobject shared by two parents (drawn when? once or twice?), z_index on a child vs parent, stroke_behind interleaving (fill/stroke split ordering), add/remove/bring_to_front/bring_to_back effects on stable order, and the depth-test partition boundary (painter order within non-depth-tested; per-tile depth among depth-tested).

Generate the fixture expectations from the pinned Reference's batching code by INSPECTION (its semantics, not its pixel output), recording each ruling with a source citation — this is the kind of archaeology that must be done once, in one bead, not rediscovered per renderer bug.

Acceptance: the ordering-trace corpus green; property test (stable under no-op mutations; insertion order preserved among equal keys); the written ordering spec committed beside the fixtures (Lumen's W5 beads cite it as normative).

title: W3: Updaters, ValueTrackers, and the .animate builder — corrected where the Reference is buggy
description:
Implement the dynamic-behavior surface (§8.6): insertion-ordered dt and non-dt updaters with suspend/resume; ValueTracker / ExponentialValueTracker / ComplexValueTracker; always_redraw / f_always closure binding into the clock; and the .animate builder implementing the Reference's REAL rules — animation arguments set once per chain; override_animate methods un-chainable; dynamic target lookup at build time.

Two deliberate corrections (D-05; Appendix C rulings — each a Behavior Note): (1) add_updater(call=True) runs the update ONCE (the Reference double-calls — C-5); (2) Group.__add__ returns a NEW group like Mobject.__add__ (the Reference mutates in place on one path — C-6; consistent value semantics).

Semantics that must be exact: updater execution order is insertion order within dt and non-dt groups; the interaction with animation suspend_mobject_updating (an animated mobject's updaters pause unless opted in); updaters observing post-interpolation state at the scene level is CHOREO's frame-order business (§9.3) but the hooks live here; updater add/remove during iteration has a defined outcome (snapshot the list per tick).

Builder mechanics: recording chained calls into a deferred target (this doubles as the Rust fluent API's recording machinery per G0-1 — one implementation, two front doors); prepare_animation accepts Animation | _AnimationBuilder ONLY, rejecting bare methods precisely as the pinned Reference does (§9.1 shares this contract).

Acceptance: ordering tests (mixed dt/non-dt insertion sequences); suspend/resume matrix; the two corrections tested with Behavior Notes drafted; builder fixture corpus (chained-call recording, once-only args, override_animate no-chain, dynamic target).

title: W3: Arena snapshots & serialization — SceneState, undo, replay barriers, fixtures
description:
Implement Marionette's persistence layer (§8.7): arena snapshots in the fmn-hash canonical format (§6.7), with per-field .npy export via fnp-io for fixture interchange. Backs four consumers with one mechanism: SceneState (§13.1 — time, play count, top-level mobjects + copies, at the Reference's scope), Studio undo, replay-journal barriers (§13.4 — snapshot at a barrier so replay can restore and continue), and the Gauntlet's geometry-snapshot self-goldens (§16.3 — lifecycle-point snapshots bit-locked per platform).

Scope boundaries stated honestly (§8.7, §13.4): NO claim to serialize Python closures — updater callables snapshot as identity + version hash (the effect model's callback-hash vocabulary); restoring across a code change that alters a callback invalidates the barrier (conservative invalidation is the replay bead's rule; this bead provides the hashes).

Format requirements: versioned schema id per §6.7's policy (additive-minor / breaking-major from day one — snapshots persist in caches and repro bundles); deterministic bytes (canonical field order, NaN/-0 canonicalization at the boundary, no map-order leakage) so that snapshot-hash equality is meaningful for the replay journal and self-goldens; size limits and corruption detection (checksums) on read.

Acceptance: snapshot/restore round-trip across representative scenes (geometry + uniforms + family topology + RNG state via fmn-core); byte-determinism test (same state ⇒ same bytes, twice, and across a re-open); versioning negative test (future-major refused cleanly); .npy field export readable by NumPy in a fixture-generation script.

title: W4: The Animation contract — constructor surface, lifecycle, lag semantics, prepare_animation
description:
Implement the Animation base contract exactly (§9.1, §1.3): the constructor surface field-for-field — (mobject, run_time=1.0, time_span, lag_ratio, rate_func=smooth, remover, final_alpha_value, suspend_mobject_updating) — the begin → interpolate(alpha) → finish lifecycle, and per-submobject lag via get_sub_alpha (the exact formula: lag distributes alpha across the family by lag_ratio, per the pinned source).

Sharp edges that are contract, not detail: prepare_animation accepts Animation | _AnimationBuilder ONLY and rejects bare methods, precisely as the pinned Reference does (coordinate with W3's builder bead — one recording mechanism serves both front doors); time_span re-windows an animation inside its group interval; final_alpha_value governs the state finish() leaves behind; remover triggers scene removal at finish; suspend_mobject_updating pauses the target's own updaters during the animation unless opted out (interlocks with W3's updater bead and W4's frame-order bead).

Rate-function plumbing: rate_func composes with squish_rate_func and time_span through ONE normalized-alpha pipeline (§9.4) — implement the normalization once, here, so every mechanism family and composition operator shares it; fixture the tricky compositions (squish of squish, time_span inside LaggedStart).

Acceptance: lifecycle-order tests (begin exactly once, interpolate monotone alpha sequence per §9.2's sample points, finish exactly once even under skip); get_sub_alpha fixture corpus generated from the Reference formula across lag_ratio values and family sizes; prepare_animation rejection tests; the normalized-alpha pipeline property tests (composition associativity where defined).

title: W4: The RationalFrameClock — manim's exact sample points, drift-free
description:
Implement the clock (§9.2, D-07, BN-02): an i64 frame index over fps, computing time as exact rationals — emitting the SAME nominal sample sequence as the Reference's arange(0, run_time, 1/fps) + 1/fps semantics: no emitted alpha-zero progression frame (begin() interpolates at zero separately, outside the emission sequence); upward duration rounding (a run_time that is not a whole number of frames still covers its end); the final sample may exceed run_time (alpha clamps per the Reference's rule); finish() applies final_alpha_value. Scene code experiences manim's timing model without manim's float drift — hour-long renders accumulate zero error (Behavior Note BN-02).
