Why rational: the Reference advances a float accumulator; at 30fps an hour of frames drifts visibly, and two runs disagree with themselves under different chunkings. Our clock is (frame_index, fps) — time derives, never accumulates. Every subsystem that needs "now" (updaters' dt, wait_until predicates, presenter holds, A/V sync in Reel) reads this clock; dt is exactly 1/fps as a rational, converted to f64 at the consumer boundary.

PERMANENT REFUSAL (D-18, §10.5): adaptive or variable frame sampling is refused forever — the sample points are semantics, and every scheduler freedom in §17 is defined relative to them. Encode this in the type: there is no API to emit off-grid samples.

Acceptance: sample-sequence fixtures vs the Reference formula across awkward run_times (0.1, 1/3, 2.9999, exact multiples); drift test (10^6 frames: exact equality with closed-form time); wait/wait_until/skip interaction tests; the no-off-grid-API property documented and asserted.

title: W4: The six-step frame order + the FramePacket freeze
description:
Implement the frame loop's load-bearing order EXACTLY (§9.3, §1.4, D-19): (1) animation update_mobjects(dt) → (2) animation interpolate(alpha) → (3) time advances → (4) SCENE updaters run, observing post-interpolation state → (5) capture → (6) emit. always_redraw-style scenes semantically depend on step 4 seeing step 2's output; this order is a contract, not an implementation choice.

The UPDATE-ORDER CORPUS locks it: dt-updaters on animated mobjects; target-with-updater; suspend both ways (animation suspending target updaters; explicit suspend_updating); wait and wait_until; skip mode; final samples; unequal run_times in one play(); time_span re-windowing. Each corpus scene asserts observable state at specific frames — these are semantic regression tests, not pixel tests.

THE FRAMEPACKET (Rev 4's boundary at step 5, reordering nothing): capture freezes an immutable FramePacket — frame index and alpha, camera state, RNG substream states (via §6.5's keyed forks), and revisioned references into Lumen's compiled render state — after which the frame no longer depends on mutable scene state. Everything before the freeze is the serial front-end; everything after (IR sync, binning, raster, color conversion, emission) consumes ONLY the packet. This is what makes the §17.4 pipeline and §9.5 frame-parallelism legal. Design the packet as genuinely immutable (no interior mutability, revision handles not live pointers) and cheap (CoW snapshot references, not deep copies).

Acceptance: the update-order corpus green; FramePacket immutability enforced by construction (API review + a test that mutating scene state after capture cannot affect a held packet); packet-cost measurement (O(touched-state), suitable for 3-6 in flight per §17.4).

title: W4: Five mechanism families → the 80 animation classes + the align_data fixture corpus
description:
Implement the animation catalog the way the plan factors it (§9.4, §1.3): FIVE mechanism families implemented once, well — (1) Transform-family: family alignment + field lerp through path_func (straight, arc, clockwise/counterclockwise variants); (2) partial-reveal (ShowCreation/Uncreate/Write/DrawBorderThenFill, driven by true proportion via W2's arclength layer); (3) fade/grow (with shift/scale composition, FadeTransform's cross-fade mechanics); (4) indication composites (Indicate, Flash, FocusOn, ApplyWave, WiggleOutThenIn, passing-flash family); (5) functional maps (Homotopy, ComplexHomotopy, PhaseFlow, MoveAlongPath — the latter constant-speed by W2ARC, BN-03). Then ALL 80 classes from Appendix A's animation modules as parameterizations of those mechanisms, each mapped in the Parity Ledger — including the transform zoo (ApplyMethod/ApplyMatrix/ApplyComplexFunction/CyclicReplace/Swap/Restore...), creation/fading/growing/indication/movement/numbers/rotation/specialized/update modules, and TurnInsideOut with C-1's ruling (the Reference calls a nonexistent method; we implement the evident intent — Behavior Note).

THE ALIGN CORPUS: the align_data family-alignment path (null-padding, point resampling, family-shape reconciliation between source and target) under a 1000-pair fixture corpus — STRUCTURAL assertions: point counts, family shapes, interpolation endpoints — generated across the mobject-type matrix (VMobject×VMobject with differing subpath counts, group×single, text×shape...). Alignment bugs are the classic manim wart; the corpus is the wall against regressions.

Acceptance: mechanism unit tests per family; all 80 classes constructible with Reference-default parameters and Ledger-mapped; the align corpus green; TransformMatchingParts/Shapes present (TransformMatchingTex/Strings land with W6's span maps — leave the seam explicit).

title: W4: Composition & timeline algebra — AnimationGroup/Succession/LaggedStart + the declarative Timeline
description:
Implement animation composition (§9.4): AnimationGroup / Succession / LaggedStart / LaggedStartMap over (start, end) interval math — group run_time derivation, lag_ratio spreading across members, nested composition (groups of groups with independent rate_funcs), squish_rate_func and time_span flowing through the single normalized-alpha pipeline (W4CONTRACT owns the pipeline; this bead exercises its composition surface hard). Interval semantics are the Reference's: members map their (start,end) sub-intervals onto parent alpha; a member's rate_func applies inside its window.

THE DECLARATIVE TIMELINE: keyframes, labels, seek — compiling to the SAME primitives (an authored Timeline is sugar over composed animations on the rational clock, never a second engine). This is the substrate for Studio scrubbing (§13.5) and the WASM tier-2 serialized-timeline player (§10.7): a Timeline must serialize (fmn-hash canonical format) with enough fidelity that the player replays it without scene code. Seek semantics: seeking to time t reconstructs state deterministically — for pure segments via snapshot + interpolate-at-alpha; across stateful segments via the nearest checkpoint + replay (coordinate with §13.4's journal; the Timeline records segment boundaries).

Acceptance: interval-math fixtures vs the Reference (nested groups, lag interactions, unequal member run_times); normalized-alpha composition tests shared with W4CONTRACT; Timeline round-trip (author → serialize → load → replay ⇒ identical frame states); seek determinism tests (seek(t) ≡ play-through-to(t) for pure content).

title: W4: The segment-purity classifier — automatic, conservative, journaled
description:
Implement segment purity (§9.5, D-19, R20): Choreo classifies EVERY play()/wait() segment automatically by its effect signature (vocabulary shared with §13.4's effect model). A segment is PURE when each of its frames is a function of the begin-state CoW snapshot and its alpha alone: no dt-updaters, no always_redraw/f_always closures, no stateful tracers (TracedPath, TracingTail, AnimatedBoundary), no scene updaters, no callbacks with unclassified effects. Pure segments render embarrassingly parallel across frames: worker k reconstructs frame state from snapshot + alpha(k) + the KEYED per-frame RNG fork (§6.5 — state derived from (substream, frame index), never sequential pulls), rasterizes, and hands the frame to the ordered emitter, which sequences by frame index regardless of completion order. Stateful segments serialize their front-end and lean on the §17.4 pipeline instead.

THE CONSERVATIVE RULE (R20, load-bearing): misclassification would be a CORRECTNESS bug, so any unrecognized effect demotes the segment to stateful. The classifier's effect vocabulary is a closed allowlist of provably-pure constructs; everything else — unknown updater shapes, Python callbacks without a purity declaration, custom animation classes overriding interpolate with side effects — demotes. Classifications are RECORDED per segment in the replay journal, so a misclassification found later is diagnosable and its effect class is demoted engine-wide until root-caused (R20's kill rule).

On an 8-core laptop this collapses to ordinary behavior; on a 96-core workstation it is near-linear — the single biggest CPU lever in the plan (§17's scaling hierarchy).

Acceptance: classification unit tests across the effect matrix (each impure construct demotes; each pure construct passes); an end-to-end equivalence test — a pure segment rendered serially vs frame-parallel at {1,4,16} workers produces BIT-IDENTICAL frames (this is PG-5's per-commit core); journal-recording tests; a demotion-path test proving unknown effects demote.

title: W5: The compiled render IR — retained tables, independent revisions, primitive hints, glyph instancing
description:
Implement Lumen's retained core (§10.8, D-20). Between Marionette's authoritative state and the execution engines sits a compiled, backend-neutral render IR: PathTable, SegmentTable, StyleTable, InstanceTable, ImageTable, and per-tile command lists — synchronized LAZILY from the RecordBuffer under §8.2's mirror rule, with backend-specific layouts derived from it (SoA/AoSoA sized to SIMD lanes on CPU; device arrays for the annex).

INDEPENDENT REVISIONS per renderable resource — topology, geometry, transform, style, visibility/order, image, camera-projection — and the distinctions pay directly: a color change does not regenerate curve coefficients; a translation does not recompute an object-space arc-length table; a camera move does not re-decode font outlines. Each COMPILED PATH retains: y-monotone quadratic splits (at vertical-tangent parameters), screen-independent coefficients, object- and screen-space bounds, the arc-length LUT (shared artifact with W2ARC), conservative stroke slabs, fill winding metadata, its primitive hint, previous tile coverage, and old + new dirty bounds.

PRIMITIVE HINTS route semantic shapes off the general quadratic solver: line/polyline → capsule-distance kernel; circle/arc → direct arc kernel; rectangle/rounded-rect → specialized coverage; dot → radial kernel; image → sampler kernel; nearly-linear quadratic → line fast path under an explicit screen-space error bound; glyph → cached path instance. Mutation through set_points or a writable live view invalidates the hint back to the general path — correctness never depends on a hint.

GLYPH/SHAPE INTERNING: an outline compiles once (content-addressed with the §11.3 span machinery and fmn-cache) and each occurrence is an INSTANCE (transform, color, clip, order) — for text-heavy mathematical scenes this deletes the bulk of geometry duplication and binning work; the same mechanism serves dots, arrow tips, repeated graph nodes, copied decorations.

G0-8's mapping report feeds this design (device-layout implications discovered there land here, not at W9-time).

Acceptance: revision-independence tests (each mutation class touches exactly its revision); lazy-sync tests (untouched resources never recompile); hint-invalidation tests; instancing dedup test (N copies of a glyph ⇒ 1 compiled path + N instances); IR golden snapshots for fixture scenes.

title: W5: Two-level binning, per-tile command lists, painter-order-safe occlusion pruning
description:
Implement Lumen's spatial organization (§10.8): object bounds → ~128x128 MACROTILES → engine-sized fine tiles → stable per-tile command runs, with sizes tuned per platform by the ExecutionPlan (§17.4) — tuning changes speed, never semantics. Command lists keep STABLE draw indices derived from W3ORDER's deterministic draw sequence; transparent runs stay in EXACT painter order; (annex note for later: deterministic count/prefix/scatter or stable key sort, never unordered atomic appends — the CPU binning must be expressible that way too, so keep the algorithm order-stable by construction).

OCCLUSION PRUNING, painter-order-safe: a conservative back-to-front opaque-coverage mask per tile lets provably-hidden commands be skipped ONLY when the result is provably unchanged (full opaque cover by later-drawn content in the same tile, no blending interactions); depth-tested 3D adds a hierarchical per-tile depth summary. The rule is absolute: pruning is an optimization with a proof obligation per skip — when in doubt, draw.

Determinism obligations (§10.5): tiles are write-disjoint by construction (one owner per tile per pass); composite order is fixed; binning output is identical at any thread count (parallel binning must produce the same command lists as serial — stable partitioning, no completion-order dependence).

Acceptance: binning golden tests (fixture scenes ⇒ identical tile command lists at {1,4,16} threads); painter-order preservation tests through binning; occlusion-pruning equivalence tests (pruned vs unpruned frames bit-identical on an adversarial corpus: translucent stacks, opaque covers, depth-tested mixes); macrotile/fine-tile size-sweep test proving size choices don't change output bits.

title: W5: Analytic fill — nonzero-winding coverage evaluated on the curves
description:
Implement the fill rasterizer (§10.2): tiled scanline nonzero-winding coverage evaluated ANALYTICALLY on the quadratics — segments y-monotonized by splitting at the vertical-tangent parameter (retained in the compiled path, W5IR); per scanline, exact segment intersections from the closed-form quadratic root; signed trapezoidal area accumulation per cell. No triangulation, no signed-alpha tricks, no orientation bookkeeping — the mathematics rasterized directly, which is the whole point of owning the renderer (Appendix B: the Reference's fill is a stack of GPU workarounds we decline to copy).

Perspective handled honestly: projected quadratics are RATIONAL in screen space, so 3D paths are evaluated in homogeneous coordinates or adaptively subdivided to a stated tolerance — never silently treated as affine.

Interior color for per-vertex fill_rgba gradients uses a DEFINED interpolation field: arc-length-parameterized boundary interpolation with mean-value coordinates in the interior — specified, tested, stable under subdivision (subdividing a path must not change its gradient). Winding-consistent self-intersection behavior (nonzero rule, matching the defined model); fill_border_width as a principled inner border stroke (GL_MAX composite hack retired).

Fast paths (§10.2's closing rule): the general machinery is the FALLBACK, not the toll road — primitive hints (W5IR) route lines/circles/rects/dots/glyphs to specialized kernels; interiors of fully-covered tiles fill as vectorized spans; the scanline evaluator computes exact coverage only where edges actually are.

Acceptance: coverage oracle tests vs analytically-known areas (circles, polygons, overlapping windings) at sub-1/256-px error; subdivision-invariance metamorphic tests (fill result invariant under curve subdivision — geometry AND gradient); self-intersection fixtures; homogeneous-path tests vs high-resolution subdivision reference; primitive-kernel vs general-path equivalence tests; look-study (G0-2) calibration constants applied.

title: W5: Strokes — true curve-distance, round caps, principled joins, arclength width
description:
Implement the stroke rasterizer (§10.3): exact/high-accuracy signed distance to the quadratic within conservative slabs (slabs retained per compiled path, W5IR); ROUND CAPS on open ends (the Reference's butt caps were a ribbon-pipeline artifact; round is the deliberate choice, Behavior-Noted with BN-06); round/bevel/miter joins with a real miter limit plus the smooth "auto" join tuned in G0-2's look study; width and color interpolated by ARC LENGTH (via the shared LUT), not parameter space — a stroke whose width varies does so uniformly along the curve, as the eye expects; flat_stroke vs camera-facing 3D stroke construction both supported (the uniform flows from W3FAMILY's inventory); the smoothstep AA profile at the calibrated ~1.5px weight.

Every one of these is visibly better than the ≤32-segment polyline ribbons it replaces — which is the point (§1.5 ↷). The quality bar is judged in the Look Gallery against Reference imagery: same feel, cleaner.

Numerical care: distance-to-quadratic is a cubic root-find — use the stable closed-form/iterative hybrid with the degenerate cases (colinear control points, cusp-adjacent) routed to the precision-exception path (§6.1); certified mode runs the canonical arithmetic (W5CERT decides representation per G0-6).

Performance shape: stroke SDF + AA resolve is the dominant cost of typical 2D scenes (per the plan's annex ROI ranking) — structure the kernel for the SIMD hot list (§17.3: stroke-SDF slabs are named) and for the annex offload (G0-8 proved this exact stage on Metal). Scalar-complete first; the structure must not preclude lanes.

Acceptance: distance-accuracy oracle tests vs brute-force sampling; cap/join golden fixtures (every combination, including miter-limit escapes to bevel); arclength-width metamorphic test (reparameterized curve ⇒ identical stroke); degenerate-case corpus; Look Gallery review of the stroke calibration set.

title: W5: 3D, camera, lighting, textures — the kept look, ported exactly
description:
Implement the 3D plane with the Reference's aesthetic DNA kept deliberately (§10.4, §10.6, §1.5, Appendix B, D-04):

CAMERA: CameraFrame on W2SPACE's fixed Rotation conventions; the Reference's projection constants ported exactly — w = 1 - z, z *= -0.1, frame rescale — because camera code and scene composition assume them; is_fixed_in_frame as a float mix (not a boolean branch); the four clip planes.

ORDERING & DEPTH: painter's order by W3ORDER's batching model; OPT-IN depth-test against per-tile f32 depth for depth-tested partitions; transparency composites in painter order within its partition.

LIGHTING: the finalize_color model ported AS-IS — shading = (reflectiveness, gloss, shadow), light at (-10, 10, 10), Surface defaults (0.3, 0.2, 0.4), dark_shift = 0.2 — because that shading IS the 3b1b look (Appendix B: kept). Surfaces: fixed UV-grid tessellation with perspective-correct interpolation. True-dot radial glow falloff kept (glow math and feel).

TEXTURES & IMAGES (§10.6): bilinear filtering with DEFINED wrap/orientation/alpha/color-space behavior; decode via fmn-codec (W8 — the PNG/JPEG bead is a dependency); exotic formats route through the ffmpeg capability with named errors.

Calibration: G0-2's look study fixes the lighting match measurement; the Look Gallery's 3D set (spheres, tori, textured surfaces, glow dots against gradients) is the standing review.

Acceptance: projection fixtures vs the Reference constants (loose f32); lighting golden renders reviewed in the Gallery; depth-partition ordering tests (mixed depth-tested/painter content); clip-plane fixtures; texture policy tests (wrap/orientation/alpha matrix); euler-singularity camera animation test (via W2SPACE).

title: W5: Adaptive coverage anti-aliasing — classify tiles, supersample only complex edges, fuse the resolve
description:
Implement Rev 4's AA scheme (§10.4, D-21). Frames render at native output resolution; tiles are CLASSIFIED (via W5IR/W5BIN metadata) as empty, fully-covered, simple-edge, or complex-edge: interiors fill as vectorized spans; analytic coverage is evaluated only near edges; 2x/4x edge supersampling applies ONLY to complex-edge cells (thin overlapping strokes, dense self-intersections, high-frequency 3D silhouettes); and the resolve is FUSED into the coverage pass so the supersampled buffer never makes a second trip through memory. Full-frame SSAA spent most of its work on pixels that were empty or fully covered; the adaptive scheme spends it where the picture is — this matters most on bandwidth-limited parts (§17.6: Apple silicon's wall is bandwidth, not FLOPs).

Contract discipline: in `standard`, the classification thresholds are a QUALITY knob (§4) — but the escalation defaults are FIXED in G0-2's look study so default output reads as "the same feel, cleaner." In `certified`, the canonical analytic path runs everywhere and classification is a scheduling choice that CANNOT change bits (§10.5) — assert this by construction: the certified engine's per-pixel result must be independent of tile class. A forced-SSAA knob remains for A/B comparison and debugging.

Acceptance: certified-invariance test (frames bit-identical with classification on/off/forced across an adversarial corpus); standard-mode quality tests vs forced-4x SSAA within the versioned visual-equivalence budget (§16.3's engine-equivalence machinery); performance evidence (measured work reduction on the gallery scenes, recorded via §17.1 instrumentation); threshold calibration constants from G0-2 applied and documented.

title: W5: The certified CPU engine — canonical raster arithmetic, the definition of the bits
description:
Implement the certified execution engine (§10.5, §6.1, §6.6, D-18; consumes G0-6's OQ-1 decision): the engine whose output IS the definition of correct bits for self-goldens, PG-5, and --reproducible.

Arithmetic: per G0-6's ruling — either floating-point with fmn-dmath transcendentals, fixed-order reductions, FMA off; or canonical fixed-point screen coordinates + integer coverage accumulation at defined precision with explicit rounding. Either way: NO fast-math, NO FMA on certified paths, reductions in fixed order and LANE-COUNT-INDEPENDENT (no horizontal sums whose association varies with vector width — the trap that silently breaks bit identity across SIMD build tiers); within the certified engine THE SCALAR PATH IS THE DEFINITION and every SIMD tier must match it bit-for-bit; degenerate/badly-conditioned cases route to the precision-exception path in every mode.

The parallelism contract, enforced here structurally (§10.5 a–f): frame bits are a pure function of (begin snapshot, alpha, frame-indexed RNG, input closure); write-disjoint tiles, fixed composite order; frame-index-ordered emission; engine/backend identity journaled into the input closure (§16.7).

Verification hooks: PG-5's thread-count sweep ({1,4,16} per commit, {32,96}+ weekly) runs THIS engine's output through the self-golden rig; the certified matrix cross-platform check (W1WCI's runners) compares frame hashes across linux-x86-64/linux-aarch64/macos-aarch64. The engine-equivalence suite (W10) holds every OTHER engine to this one's reference frames.

Acceptance: bit-identity at {1,4,16} threads on the primitive corpus; cross-platform bit-identity on the certified matrix for the G0-6 frame plus a growing corpus; scalar-vs-SIMD-tier bit-identity harness in place (tiers land in W5FAST but the harness is certified-side); the input-closure journaling path tested.

title: W5: The fast CPU engine — mixed precision + SIMD build tiers (std::simd, safe target_feature)
description:
Implement the fast CPU engine (§6.1, §17.3, D-15): the standard-mode default — mixed precision with FMA permitted, screen-space f32 under the explicit subpixel error budget (≈1/256 px initial target; exceed it and the compiler subdivides or routes to the high-precision fallback), SIMD build tiers, all held to the versioned visual-equivalence budget against certified reference frames (§10.1, §16.3) — never semantically divergent, never a separate renderer.

SIMD as a designed system (§17.3): std::simd on the pinned nightly with SAFE #[target_feature] (target-feature-1.1: annotated safe functions are safely callable when the feature is statically enabled) — every kernel stays inside #![forbid(unsafe_code)]; dispatch is by BUILD TIER (portable / x86-64-v3 / x86-64-v4 / aarch64+NEON), never per-call runtime detection; SUITE.lock's certified target-feature sets govern the tiers; per-tier artifacts ship at W11. The hot list in ROI order: point-transform passes, field lerp, scanline quadratic-root evaluation, stroke-SDF slabs, color transfer functions, PNG filter/unfilter (W8's, but the kernel idioms are established here), WAV mixing, and fmn-dmath itself (§6.6).

Ordering discipline: scalar-complete FIRST (the ordering, not the destination) — instrument (§17.1) → eliminate work (§10.8, already landed) → pipeline (§17.4) → vectorize (THIS bead) → offload. Hand-vectorizing the full-work renderer first would optimize work that should not exist.

Acceptance: engine-equivalence suite green (fast vs certified within the visual budget on the corpus); per-tier build CI (compile + test each tier; full matrix weekly per R22); measured speedups per hot-list kernel recorded via §17.1; lane-count-independence verified where kernels are shared with the certified engine.

title: W5: The retained compositor — dirty regions, tile caches, byte-identical reuse (export AND preview)
description:
Implement the retained compositor (§10.8, D-20): the dirty-region story generalized into retained rendering everywhere — export, not just preview.

Mechanics: object→tile dependencies tracked; BOTH old and new bounds dirtied on movement; completed tiles cached keyed on (command list, resource revisions, camera revision, output transform); static layer runs above/below animated content cached as units; per-frame allocation in bump arenas over pooled frame buffers — the zero-alloc steady state PG-6 measures (allocation count is a sharper regression tripwire than wall-clock).

The load-bearing property: REUSED TILES ARE BYTE-IDENTICAL, so reuse is PG-5-safe and WORKS UNDER CERTIFIED — a wait() frame reduces to clock advance plus reuse; a blinking cursor does not re-rasterize a 4K frame. This must be structural: the cache key includes every input that could change bytes (per W5IR's revision taxonomy plus camera + output transform), and a cache hit bypasses rasterization entirely rather than re-rasterizing-and-comparing.

Interaction with live views (§8.2): while a writable live NumPy view exists on an object, that object's tiles are conservatively invalidated every frame — correctness over reuse, always; ranged-edit APIs are the precise-dirty opt-in.

Acceptance: reuse-equivalence tests (cached vs cold frames bit-identical across an animation corpus with waits, static layers, moving objects); key-completeness adversarial tests (every mutation class invalidates its tiles — style, transform, order, camera, output transform each tested); the wait()-frame fast path measured (near-zero raster work via §17.1 counters); allocation-count instrumentation wired for PG-6; live-view conservative-invalidation test.

title: W5: The frame pipeline + ExecutionPlan — bounded in-flight frames, render teams, topology-aware scheduling (fmn-runtime)
description:
Implement the execution architecture (§17.4, D-19, D-24) in fmn-runtime:

THE PIPELINE: after W4ORDER's FramePacket freeze, stages overlap across frames — scene update + Python callbacks for frame N+2, IR sync + binning for N+1, rasterization for N, color conversion + sink submission for N-1 — behind a BOUNDED in-flight queue (interactive preview 1-2; ordinary offline export 3-6; high-core export enough to occupy the render teams; annex 2-4), with ordered emission (W8's emitter) and pipeline barriers from the effect model (§13.4: any operation that must observe rendered pixels drains in-flight frames; ordinary manim scene code never hits one). Pure segments (W4PURITY) additionally fan whole frames across render teams.

THE EXECUTIONPLAN: derived from fmn-platform's HardwareTopology — engine, frames in flight, render-team count and threads per team, tile/macrotile dimensions, SIMD tier, scratch sizes, output pixel format. TEAMS, NOT ONE FLAT POOL: a small latency-oriented scene/update team; one render team per in-flight frame (on a 96-core part: three 32-core or four 24-core teams, never 192 threads fighting over one frame — OQ-11 owns the sizing priors); an output team on SMT siblings (they don't compete for vector ports). Within a team: physical cores first, SMT after measurement; one scratch arena per worker; local work queues, one owner per tile; no global hot atomics; coarse stealing before crossing CCD/NUMA domains; frame buffers first-touched by their team.

Mode rule: `standard` caches autotuning under the hardware fingerprint; `certified` runs its fixed declared configuration — and NO schedule can change bits, because §10.5's contract binds every one (asserted by PG-5's sweeps). asupersync NEVER enters the frame loop (multi-scene parallelism is the batch feature only).

Acceptance: pipeline-equivalence tests (pipelined vs serial execution ⇒ bit-identical frames, all queue depths); barrier tests (a pixel-observing operation drains correctly); ExecutionPlan derivation tests over fixture topologies (8-core laptop, 96-core dual-CCD, >64-CPU Windows groups, Apple P/E); team scheduling smoke tests with utilization counters (§17.1); latency measurement for the preview depth-1-2 configuration.

title: W5: WASM tier 1 — the frame renderer in the browser
description:
Implement WASM tier 1 (§10.7): the frame renderer compiled to wasm32 — single-threaded by default; threads only under atomics + cross-origin isolation — rendering scenes to canvas/ImageData in the browser. Tier 2 (the serialized-timeline player consuming W4TIMELINE's format) is a G5 deliverable; this bead makes tier 1 real and keeps the workspace honestly wasm-clean.

Scope: the wasm feature axis through fmn-core/geom/mobject/anim/render/frame (fmn-platform provides wasm capability implementations: virtual fs, performance.now clock, no process capability — the ffmpeg boundary is structurally absent and native outputs are the only sinks); wasm-bindgen surface (pre-authorized under D1) exposing scene construction + frame rendering; size discipline (feature-gate fonts/assets; measure and record the artifact size — R19's CI-measured budgets).

Depends on G0-7's wasm-target audit (which closure members compile; which need shims at fmn-platform — R15's mitigation). Determinism note: wasm is not in the certified matrix, but standard-mode determinism (same seed ⇒ same output on the same build) must hold — fmn-dmath already gives wasm the same transcendentals, which is most of the battle.

Acceptance: a browser demo rendering a primitive-corpus scene to canvas; wasm32 CI build + headless test (node/wasmtime) for the core crates; artifact-size budget recorded; single-thread determinism test (two renders, same bytes).

title: W5: The Metal annex engine — Studio preview production backend (via frankentorch)
description:
Implement the Accelerator Annex's first production engine (§10.7, §17.5, D-22; building directly on G0-8's proven kernels): Metal via frankentorch — the ONLY GPU gateway (no wgpu, ever: D1 audit catastrophe + duplicates ft) — serving Studio preview on Apple silicon, where unified memory makes the handoff nearly free and preview frames carry no bit promise.

Offload set, ranked by ROI (§10.7): stroke SDF + AA resolve (dominant 2D cost, per-pixel independent — G0-8's stage); SSAA resolve + transfer functions; DotCloud/glow splats (overdraw-heavy, additive); 3D surface tessellation with the kept lighting; large fill coverage. Platform playbook (§17.6): ft shared buffers for upload; tile-shader/threadgroup techniques keeping tile-local coverage and compositing on-chip (Apple TBDR rewards exactly this); memoryless attachments for pass-local results; threadgroup sizes from pipeline introspection, never CUDA habit; direct presentation for preview; GPU-side NV12/P010 conversion before any export readback (§14.3).

Hard boundaries (R21): standard-mode ONLY, never certified (D-18 refusal); annex binning uses deterministic count/prefix/scatter or stable key sort, never unordered atomic appends; excluded from core gates — PG-A profiles gate ANNEX changes only, so acceleration can never mask a core regression (annex work PAUSES whenever core PG-1..PG-3 regress); backend identity journaled into the input closure; held to the engine-equivalence visual budget vs certified frames.

Acceptance: Studio preview renders through the annex by default on supported hardware with the declared CPU fallback intact (G3's required-with-fallback rule, OQ-12); engine-equivalence suite green for the annex on the corpus; PG-A rig measuring fps, bytes up/back per frame, occupancy on the pinned Apple profile; fallback-path test (annex unavailable ⇒ CPU preview, no error).

title: W6: fmd-font — factor the Font module out of fmd + build the glyf outline decoder (cross-repo)
description:
Cross-repo task in franken_markdown (§2.3, §11.1, §2.9 — the upstream ledger's first seed entry). Factor fmd's text::Font sfnt machinery (cmap formats 4/12, glyf/loca, composite glyphs, hmtx, kern + focused GPOS, subsetting) into a new fmd-font workspace crate, and EXTEND it with the piece FrankenManim needs and fmd never did: the GLYF OUTLINE DECODER — simple and composite glyphs decoded to quadratic contours (TrueType outlines are already quadratic — zero-loss into our QuadPath model) with phantom-point-correct metrics (left/right side bearings, advance interaction with hmtx, composite offset/anchor semantics).

Bundled faces (§11.1): Computer Modern roman/bold/italic/bold-italic (the default face — the 3b1b typographic identity), CM Typewriter (code), IBM Plex Sans, Noto Sans Math (symbol coverage) — all OFL, all shipped in-crate; a bare install renders every built-in scene identically everywhere; NOTHING depends on host fonts (D-08). User TTFs load by path; family-name platform scanning is a convenience tier. Honestly tiered out with revisit triggers (§16.6): CFF/CFF2, variable fonts, color glyphs, GSUB ligature machinery beyond the bundled-face sets, mark positioning, bidi/complex shaping.

Security (§16.5, R14): fonts are untrusted input — bounded table sizes, offset validation, composite-recursion limits, fuzz target from day one (malicious font tables are a DoS surface even in safe Rust).

Governance (R8): fmd-font rides UPSTREAM_LEDGER.md + SUITE.lock like any foundation crate; FrankenManim CI builds both repos from the lock. G0-3 (the fmd-math spike) needs CM outlines decoded — this bead's decoder is its prerequisite.

Acceptance: outline decoding golden tests for CM glyphs (including composites and accent glyphs) as quad contours; metrics tests vs known values; round-trip through W2's QuadPath; fuzzer wired; the fmd workspace conversion done without breaking fmd's existing build (OQ-3's mechanics resolved and documented).

title: W6: fmd-math core — TeX grammar, the atom/spacing engine, style propagation
description:
Implement fmd-math's core (§11.4; architecture per G0-3's ratified spike). The language surface as ACTUALLY used (per G0-4's harvest ranking): groups, sub/superscripts, \frac/\dfrac/\tfrac/\binom, radicals with indices, \left...\right and fixed-size delimiters, accents (\hat \vec \dot \tilde \bar \overline \underline \overbrace \underbrace with annotations), big operators with limits (\sum \int \prod ...), the operator-name set, Greek + the standard symbol vocabulary (CM + Noto Math coverage), \text{...} islands, spacing commands, \mathbb/\mathcal/\mathrm/\mathbf/\mathsf/\mathtt, colors, phantoms, \stackrel/\overset/\underset.

The LAYOUT ENGINE: TeX's published mathematics — the EIGHT ATOM CLASSES (Ord/Op/Bin/Rel/Open/Close/Punct/Inner) and the inter-atom spacing table; display/text/script/scriptscript styles with correct propagation (\mathchoice-class behavior: numerators shrink, limits move, spacing changes by style). This bead is the parser + atom classification + spacing + style machinery; placement mathematics (fractions/radicals/scripts per Appendix-G parameters) is the next bead — but the ENGINE SHAPE (box/glue/kern node model or the spike's ratified alternative) is fixed here and must serve both.

Error doctrine (§11.5, R1): an unsupported construct is a PRECISE, NAMED error ("\substack is not yet supported; tier T2, tracked at ...") — never silence, never garbage; arbitrary token streams must error cleanly, never hang or garble (fuzz target, §16.5).

Acceptance: parse golden tests over the harvest's tier-1 corpus (parse success/fail exactly as tiered); atom-classification fixtures vs TeX's rules; spacing-table tests (every atom-class pair, every style); style-propagation fixtures; the unsupported-error format standardized (the ratchet bead consumes it); fuzzer wired.

title: W6: fmd-math layout — Appendix-G placement, synthesized CM math metrics, output paths
description:
Implement fmd-math's placement mathematics (§11.4): numerator/denominator shifts, rule thicknesses, sub/superscript placement (including the simultaneous-scripts kern rules), radical geometry (rule height, degree placement), big-operator limit placement, accent positioning with skew/italic-correction behavior — per the TeX Appendix-G parameter family — over a MATH-METRICS TABLE SYNTHESIZED AND HAND-CALIBRATED for the bundled faces (axis height, x-height, quad, sup/sub shift constants, radical parameters, fraction gaps) in the spirit of OpenType MATH. CM's TFM-era parameters must be reconstructed for our sfnt world: G0-3 ratified the synthesis method; this bead executes the full calibration and commits the tables as data with provenance notes.

OUTPUT: glyph outlines (via fmd-font) and rules as quadratic paths in our shared path model, with the §11.3 span map attached — consumed by FrankenManim as VMobjects AND by fmd as HTML/PDF vector output (the same engine serves every Franken document — leapfrog #4). Deterministic: same string + pack ⇒ identical paths, everywhere (fmd-math must route any transcendental through fixed implementations; layout arithmetic is exact rationals/f64 with defined rounding).

QUALITY BAR (§11.4): side-by-side indistinguishable-at-a-glance from LaTeX on the corpus, judged in the Look Gallery; spacing verified against the PUBLISHED parameters, not against pixels — the correctness spec is Knuth's, which is what makes no-LaTeX tenable (R1 mitigation).

Acceptance: placement-parameter tests vs published values (fraction shifts per style, script shifts, radical rules); golden path output for the G0-3 spike constructs + the tier-1 corpus head; determinism test (same input twice + cross-platform ⇒ identical path bytes); Gallery review session of the calibration formula set vs LaTeX renders.

title: W6: fmd-math extensions — extensible delimiters, environments, macros, preamble packs
description:
Complete fmd-math's tier-1 surface (§11.4):

EXTENSIBLE DELIMITERS + WIDE ACCENTS: assembled from CM's extension glyphs (repeatable middle segments + caps) per G0-3's ratified strategy (OQ-2), with DRAWN-PATH CONSTRUCTION as the universal fallback so NO requested size can fail — a \left( around a 12-row matrix gets a correct paren, always. \left...\right sizing math (delimiter covers content height per TeX's rules), \big/\Big/\bigg/\Bigg fixed sizes.

ENVIRONMENTS: the matrix family (matrix/pmatrix/bmatrix/vmatrix/Vmatrix), cases, array with column specs, align*-class multi-line with alignment points — column measurement, alignment, inter-row spacing per the published rules. (W7's Matrix mobject consumes the delimiter engine directly — §11.6.)

USER MACROS + PREAMBLE PACKS: \newcommand-style non-recursive substitution macros (with argument support, recursion refused with a named error); named PREAMBLE PACKS — macro/symbol bundles replacing tex_templates.yml — registered through fmn-config's registry (W1CONF), with a compatibility mapping for the common Reference templates. Pack identity participates in cache keys and the input closure (a pack change re-typesets, correctly).

Acceptance: delimiter-assembly golden paths at many sizes including past-glyph-inventory sizes (fallback engages); environment fixtures (alignment correctness, matrix spacing) vs published rules + LaTeX-visual Gallery review; macro expansion tests (arguments, nesting, recursion refusal); pack round-trip (define → use → cache-key changes on edit); tier-2 constructs from the harvest error with correct names.

title: W6: Native span maps — source-span provenance through layout (the two-render hack is dead)
description:
Implement span provenance (§11.3, D-09). The Reference obtains substring→glyph maps by rendering every string TWICE through Pango/LaTeX with injected color labels and aligning the renders via cdist + linear_sum_assignment — because its typesetters are black boxes. Ours are not: fmd-math and the fmn-text layouter emit a SEMANTIC SPAN MAP NATIVELY — every output glyph and rule carries its source-span provenance by construction, through every transformation (macro expansion maps to the expansion site's source span; a fraction rule belongs to the \frac's span; ligature/composed glyphs carry their covered range).

Consumers: isolate=, tex_to_color_map / t2c-family maps, substring slicing (Text[3:7], Tex slicing by sub-string), and TransformMatchingTex/TransformMatchingStrings consume the span map DIRECTLY — matching by source identity, not shape correlation. The assignment matcher (fsci-opt + cdist) REMAINS for shape-based matching (TransformMatchingShapes) where it genuinely belongs — that seam stays, clean.

Design requirements: spans survive fmd-math's node transformations (record source ranges on tokens at lex time; propagate through parse/layout; emit per-glyph); StringMobject's submobject-indexing conventions are the compatibility target (W6TEX consumes this to build the submobject structure users index — Text[3:7] and isolate= are load-bearing across the corpus); span maps serialize into the typeset cache entry (W8's cache) and appear in the Studio inspector (§13.5).

Acceptance: span-coverage property test (every glyph/rule has a span; spans tile the source or are explicitly synthetic); slicing fixtures matching Reference indexing behavior on representative strings; TransformMatchingTex end-to-end test through spans (with W4MECH's seam); macro-expansion span tests; inspector-format documented.

title: W6: fmn-text — shaping, markup, and layout over fmd-font (Scribe I)
description:
Implement the text plane (§11.2): native shaping — cmap→gids, kerning via kern + focused GPOS, bundled-face ligature sets OFF by default (matching the familiar manim look; opt-in flag) — greedy line breaking with manim's width semantics (Knuth–Plass as an option, never the silent default), and the manim markup tag set: <span>, <b>, <i>, <u>, <s>, <sub>, <sup>, <tt>, <big>, <small> with color attributes, plus the t2c/t2f/t2g/t2s/t2w maps, justification/indent/alignment/line-spacing at full constructor granularity.

Output contract: glyph runs as QuadPath outlines (via fmd-font's decoder) with W6SPAN's provenance attached, preserving StringMobject submobject-indexing conventions — Text[3:7] and isolate= must produce the same submobject structure users' scene code indexes (the compatibility surface here is structural, not pixel).

Font policy (D-08): the bundled default face renders identically on every machine; user TTFs by path; the family-name scan is a convenience tier that never silently substitutes (a missing font is a named capability-style error, not a fallback surprise). Default text face decision: the Reference defaults to Consolas via Pango — ours is the bundled default face, Behavior-Noted (BN-05 covers native-typesetting metric differences).

Markup parsing is untrusted-adjacent: bounded nesting/size, precise diagnostics, fuzz target (§16.5).

Acceptance: shaping fixtures (kern pairs, ligature on/off) for the bundled faces; line-breaking fixtures vs manim width semantics; markup-parse golden tests incl. malformed-input diagnostics; t2c-family application tests via span maps; submobject-indexing fixtures vs Reference conventions; Gallery review of text samples (BN-05 drafted).

title: W6: fmn-tex — Tex/TexText mobjects, typeset caching, the pre-play preflight (Scribe II)
description:
Implement the manim-facing math surface (§11.4-11.5): Tex and TexText mobjects consuming fmd-math — string → typeset paths → VMobject family with W6SPAN's map defining the submobject structure (SingleStringTex/OldTex-class indexing conventions preserved where the Ledger requires); isolate= and tex_to_color_map through spans; TexText's text-mode surface at its tier-1 breadth (OQ-4 resolves here: document exactly which text-mode constructs tier 1 covers).

CACHING: typeset results content-addressed in fmn-cache (W8CACHE) — keyed on (string, preamble pack hash, font hashes, fmd-math version) per §14.4; a cache hit returns paths + span map without re-layout; the cache key participates in the input closure so certified renders are cache-consistent by construction.

THE PREFLIGHT (Rev 4, §11.5 — PG-4's design mechanism): before the first play(), the runtime walks the constructed scene and preflights every static Tex/Text string across the worker pool — parallel typesetting, parallel glyph-outline decode, fmn-cache warmed — so cold-start hits PG-4's <150ms budget by design and PG-7's cached-path numbers (<100µs) are the common case. Coordinate with W9's scene runtime for the walk hook.

Error surface: an unsupported construct error from fmd-math propagates with its construct name and tier — the user sees "\substack is not yet supported (tier T2, tracked at ...)" at construction time, never a blank render.

Acceptance: Tex→VMobject structural fixtures (family shapes, submobject counts for corpus strings); cache round-trip + key-sensitivity tests (pack/font/version changes miss correctly); preflight test (N strings typeset in parallel, cache warm before first frame; measured via §17.1 phases); error-propagation tests; OQ-4's tier-1 text-mode surface documented.

title: W6: The public coverage ratchet — occurrence-weighted fmd-math coverage as a headline metric
description:
Implement the coverage ratchet (§11.5, §18; R1's public mitigation). With no LaTeX fallback, coverage DISCIPLINE replaces fallback discipline — and it must be public, precise, and monotone.

Mechanics: against G0-4's frozen corpus (the ratchet's denominator), compute per-construct and aggregate coverage — OCCURRENCE-WEIGHTED (what fraction of real-world formula mass typesets) and UNIQUE-STRING (what fraction of distinct formulas), with PARSE-vs-LAYOUT success split out (a string that parses but mislays counts as layout-fail, not success). Wire as: (a) a CI job producing the numbers per commit; (b) a RATCHET RULE — coverage may never decrease (a regression is a build break; deliberate tier changes require updating a committed baseline with a written note); (c) the public dashboard artifact (§18: a first-class DX artifact — badge + per-construct table + trend, generated into the repo/docs), naming which T2 constructs are pending with their tracked issue links, honoring the licensing rule from G0-4 (publish numbers and construct names, never the 3b1b-authored strings).

Also here: the per-construct named-error audit — every construct in the harvest that is NOT tier-1 must produce its precise unsupported error with tier + tracking link (test the error text against the harvest inventory, so no construct ever fails silently).

Acceptance: ratchet CI green with committed baseline; a deliberate-regression negative test (removing a construct fails CI); dashboard artifact generated and rendered; error-audit test over the full harvest construct list; G2's coverage checkpoint criteria (R1: coverage misses gate → public amendment with construct-sprint plan) documented as the escalation path.

title: W7: The geometry lineage — Arc/Line/Arrow/polygon families under one arc-density rule
description:
Implement the geometry classes of Appendix A (§12.1): the Arc lineage (Arc, ArcBetweenPoints, CurvedArrow/CurvedDoubleArrow, Circle, Dot/SmallDot, Ellipse, AnnularSector, Sector, Annulus) under ONE clean arc-density rule — the Reference has THREE mutually inconsistent conventions for how many curves an arc of given angle gets; ours is one documented rule (Behavior Note BN-09, drafted in W2PATH, applied here); the Line lineage (Line, DashedLine, TangentLine, Elbow, Arrow, StrokeArrow, Vector) with the TIP-ATTACHMENT ALGEBRA placing by TRUE arc length (W2ARC — tips sit at the actual end of the actual curve, dashes distribute by true length); CubicBezier (through W2CUBIC's converter); Polygon/Polyline/RegularPolygon/Triangle; ArrowTip; Rectangle/Square/RoundedRectangle; TipableVMobject as the shared tip machinery.

Compatibility method: constructors are LOCKED BY STRUCTURAL FIXTURES against the Reference where formulas intentionally coincide — point counts, layouts, dimensions (loose f32 per §16.4) — generated from the pinned Reference by the W1GAUNT extraction scripts. Where our rule deliberately differs (arc density, true-length dash placement), fixtures assert OUR documented behavior and the Behavior Note covers migration.

Primitive hints (§10.8): these constructors are exactly the shapes Lumen's hint system fast-paths — Circle/Arc/Rectangle/Dot constructors must tag their paths with the semantic hint so W5IR routes them off the general solver (and mutation clears the tag, tested).

Acceptance: structural fixture corpus green across the lineage; tip-placement tests on curved paths (true-length positions); dash-distribution tests; hint-tagging tests per constructor; BN-09 finalized with examples.

title: W7: Atlas — coordinate systems, axes, plotting (native text throughout)
description:
Implement the coordinate-system plane (§12.2): the CoordinateSystem protocol — c2p/p2c, input_to_graph_point, graphing with DISCONTINUITY handling (the Reference's recursive discontinuity search semantics), Riemann rectangles, get_area_under_graph — Axes/ThreeDAxes/NumberPlane/ComplexPlane/NumberLine with tick and number placement LOCKED BY GLYPH-SEQUENCE TESTS (all number labels are native DecimalNumber text now — W7DETEX/W6TEXT — so the test asserts the glyph sequence and positions, not pixels); UnitInterval/Slider; ParametricCurve with the Reference's t-range/epsilon semantics; FunctionGraph; ImplicitFunction over W2FIELD's isolines (min_depth/max_quads budget semantics honored).

Care points: axis number placement interacts with tick spacing, decimal formatting (DecimalNumber's edge cases — trailing zeros, negative zero, num_decimal_places), and direction/buff conventions — all locked by fixtures because plotting scenes are the corpus's bread and butter; ComplexPlane's coordinate labels (imaginary unit formatting); NumberPlane's background/faded-line families (structural fixtures for the line counts and spacing); ThreeDAxes' interaction with the kept camera model (W53D).

Graphing determinism: same function + range + config ⇒ identical sample points and identical discontinuity decisions (sampling policy documented; any adaptive step is deterministic).

Acceptance: c2p/p2c round-trip property tests; graphing fixtures incl. discontinuous functions (1/x, tan, step) matching documented semantics; glyph-sequence tests for axis numbering across ranges/scales; Riemann/area structural fixtures; ImplicitFunction budget tests.

title: W7: The de-TeX'd natives — Brace, Matrix delimiters, Checkmark/Exmark, DecimalNumber
description:
Implement §11.6/§12.3's de-TeXing — the Reference routes several NON-math classes through LaTeX because LaTeX was there; we make them native and better, removing the deepest early typesetting dependency from the library's critical path:

BRACE becomes a PARAMETRIC PATH GENERATOR — correct at any width (the Reference renders \underbrace{\qquad} and stretches it, distorting the curl at extreme widths; ours is a proper parametric family), with BraceLabel/BraceText composing with Scribe text; LineBrace orientation semantics preserved. MATRIX delimiters use fmd-math's extensible-delimiter engine DIRECTLY (W6MATHEXT) — Matrix/DecimalMatrix/IntegerMatrix/TexMatrix/MobjectMatrix lay out their element grids natively (row/col spacing, alignment per the Reference's conventions) with delimiters that scale correctly by construction. CHECKMARK/EXMARK draw from bundled glyphs (no pifont). DECIMALNUMBER/INTEGER are PURE NATIVE TEXT — one face, GLYPH-RECYCLING updates (a ticking counter re-uses glyph instances, W5IR's interning — no per-frame typesetting), correct formatting edge cases (negative zero, rounding mode, trailing zeros, unit suffixes, edge_to_fix semantics for ChangingDecimal).

Also here (§12.3): SurroundingRectangle/BackgroundRectangle/Cross/Underline; BulletedList/Title on Scribe; the interactive.py control mobjects (Button, Checkbox, sliders, ControlPanel...) as compositions bound to Proscenium events (event wiring lands with W9EVENTS; construct them here).

Behavior Note BN-08: de-TeX'd classes render from native constructions; metrics differ from the LaTeX-derived originals; scaling behavior is better-defined.

Acceptance: Brace parametric tests across widths (smooth, symmetric, correct curl); Matrix layout fixtures (spacing/alignment vs Reference conventions) with delimiter-scaling tests; DecimalNumber formatting edge-case corpus + glyph-recycling test (update does not re-typeset); structural fixtures for the remaining classes; BN-08 drafted.

title: W7: 3D solids, surfaces, fields — StreamLines on a real integrator
description:
Implement the 3D and field classes (§12.4): the three_dimensions census (Sphere, Torus, Cylinder, Cone, Line3D, Disk3D, Square3D, Cube, Prism, VGroup3D, VCube, VPrism, Dodecahedron, Prismify, SurfaceMesh) with ParametricSurface's UV-grid semantics (resolution, epsilon partial-derivative conventions) exact; Surface/TexturedSurface/SGroup over W53D's tessellation + kept lighting; TexturedGeometry with C-4's ruling (the Reference's dead triple-read not replicated).

FIELDS: VectorField/TimeVaryingVectorField — sampling semantics, magnitude→length mapping with tanh normalization ON FMN-DMATH (certified-stable), color mapping via owned colormaps (W1CORE); StreamLines on FSCI-INTEGRATE'S ADAPTIVE RK45 WITH DENSE OUTPUT — the obligation is a GOOD integrator tuned for visual quality (solve_ivp emulation is dead, §2.2), seeded from the single RNG's named substream (§6.5 — stream-line seeding must not perturb other subsystems' draws), with AnimatedStreamLines' flow animation; TracedPath/TracingTail tracing by TRUE length (W2ARC), AnimatedBoundary — all three are STATEFUL tracers, and their effect signatures must register with W4PURITY's classifier (they demote segments; that wiring is part of this bead's contract).

DotCloud/TrueDot/GlowDots/GlowDot with the kept radial glow falloff; PMobject/PGroup; ImageMobject via fmn-codec (W8); ThreeDModel via the OBJ-subset reader (vertices/faces/normals subset, documented limits — displaces trimesh/pywavefront).

Acceptance: solid constructor structural fixtures; ParametricSurface UV fixtures; StreamLines determinism test (same seed ⇒ same lines) + dense-output smoothness review in the Gallery; tracer purity-registration tests (a TracedPath scene classifies stateful); OBJ reader fixtures + fuzz entry; glow falloff Gallery check.

title: W7: Enhanced-tier Graph & Data mobjects (fnx / fp) — leapfrog content, never gate-blocking
description:
Implement the enhanced tier (§12.5-12.6, §2.4-2.5; leapfrogs #7-8): GRAPH MOBJECTS on franken_networkx — Graph/DiGraph mobjects over fnx structures, layout kernels AUDITED (or upstreamed per §2.9) with EXPLICIT determinism rules before anything enters certified renders (fnx layout must be seeded + ordered: group/sort determinism documented per kernel; any kernel that can't commit routes to a deterministic fallback layout); traversal/flow/layout-transition animations (BFS/DFS wavefronts, edge-flow pulses, layout interpolation through the Transform machinery). DATA MOBJECTS on frankenpandas — CSV ingestion, TableMobject through Scribe layout (§11.7 adjacency), BarChart/SampleSpace (Appendix A's probability module) fed from fp frames.

Tier discipline (the constitutional bit): enhanced-tier content NEVER blocks core gates — these beads land after their dependencies at leisure, and a slip here never delays G1-G4. But when they DO land, they meet the same bars: structural fixtures, determinism rules, Ledger rows, self-goldens.

Also staged here: NeuralNetworkMobject on real ft-nn modules (§2.6's content role — G5-adjacent, backlog priority within this bead's scope note) and MarkdownMobject (§11.7: fmd parser + Scribe + fmd-math inline math — documents and slides as animatable content) as the enhanced tier's follow-ons; file separate beads when scheduled.

Acceptance: fnx layout determinism tests per audited kernel (same graph + seed ⇒ same positions); graph animation fixtures; CSV→TableMobject end-to-end with Scribe layout fixtures; BarChart structural fixtures vs Reference conventions; Ledger rows marked enhanced-tier.

title: W7: The drawings shelf — whimsy classes, one self-golden each
description:
Port the drawings module (§12.7, Appendix A's svg/drawings census): Lightbulb, Speedometer, Laptop, VideoIcon/VideoSeries, Clock/ClockPassesTime, Bubble/SpeechBubble/ThoughtBubble (+ Old/Double variants), VectorizedEarth, Piano/Piano3D, DieFace, Dartboard — plus the former Tex-backed members now native via W7DETEX's machinery (Checkmark/Exmark shipped there; anything else TeX-flavored in the shelf gets the same treatment).

These are PURE CONSUMERS of Marionette + Chisel + Scribe — no new mechanisms allowed (if a drawing needs a capability the stack lacks, that's a bug in the stack's beads, not license for a one-off here). Ported LATE by design (backlog priority): they validate the stack's expressiveness and fill out the census, nothing depends on them.

Quality bar: ONE SELF-GOLDEN EACH — every class gets a bit-locked render in the self-golden corpus (cheap, and it catches regressions in the underlying machinery through real compositions); structural fixtures where the Reference's construction formulas coincide; Ledger rows for each class.

Acceptance: all census classes constructible with Reference-default parameters; one self-golden per class; Ledger updated; any deliberate visual divergence (fonts, de-TeX'd elements) Behavior-Noted by reference to existing notes.

title: W8: fmn-frame — frame buffers, pixel formats, transfer functions
description:
Implement the frame substrate (§14.1): buffer types over pooled allocations (the preallocated ring the emitter and pipeline ride — no frame-sized allocation on the hot path, PG-6's zero-steady-state-alloc rule); pixel formats — RGBA8/BGRA8, RGBA16F intermediates where quality demands, NV12/P010 video planes (with correct chroma siting and range semantics), canonical RGBA for certified output; transfer functions applied ONCE, natively, at the defined point (the ffmpeg boundary's "no obligatory eq" rule depends on us owning transfer correctly); row-stride discipline (sinks negotiate stride; internal buffers may pad).

Format conversion kernels: linear-light RGBA16F → sRGB RGBA8; RGBA → NV12/P010 (BT.709 matrix, defined rounding — these are §17.3 hot-list adjacent: color transfer functions are named); orientation is ALWAYS output orientation — fmn-frame renders the right way up and no vflip exists anywhere in the system (D-23).

Certified interaction: canonical RGBA output bytes are part of the certified artifact set — conversion kernels on the certified path use fixed-order, defined-rounding arithmetic (W5CERT's rules extend here); NV12/P010 conversions are standard-mode (they feed ffmpeg, whose products are uncertified by construction).

Acceptance: format round-trip tests (within documented precision per conversion); NV12/P010 golden vectors vs reference matrices; pool reuse tests (steady-state render loop performs zero frame allocations — instrumented via §17.1); stride/negotiation unit tests; certified-conversion determinism tests.

title: W8: fmn-codec I — PNG (full matrix) + JPEG decode
description:
Implement the primary image codecs (§14.2; displaces Pillow for the native set):

PNG DECODE across the real ecosystem: grayscale ± alpha, indexed + tRNS, 8/16-bit, Adam7 interlacing, gamma/sRGB chunk policy (defined precedence, documented), canonical-RGBA normalization, decompression-bomb rejection (declared size limits BEFORE inflate; bounded chunk counts). PNG ENCODE: stills, sequences, the Studio stream — with the owned DEFLATE implementation (governed closure: no flate2/zlib) tuned for the streaming case.

JPEG DECODE: baseline + progressive, all common subsampling (4:4:4/4:2:2/4:2:0), restart markers, EXIF orientation honored, CMYK policy defined (convert or reject, documented). No JPEG encode (not needed; exports are PNG/video).

Both decoders are UNTRUSTED-INPUT parsers (§16.5, R14): resource budgets asserted under fuzzing (must error within budget, never hang, never overallocate); fuzz targets registered from day one.

Interlock: the owned DEFLATE must support FIXED BLOCK BOUNDARIES for W8CODEC2's deterministic parallel encode — design the compressor API now (explicit flush points, reproducible bit output for a given input + parameters) so the parallel path is a composition, not a fork.

Acceptance: PNG decode conformance over the full color-type matrix (PngSuite-class corpus, committed); encode-decode round-trips; JPEG conformance corpus incl. progressive + restart + EXIF; bomb-rejection tests within budget; fuzzers wired; DEFLATE determinism test (same input/params ⇒ same bytes).

title: W8: fmn-codec II — GIF, y4m, WAV, deterministic parallel PNG sequences, SVG export
description:
Complete the native codec set (§14.2):

GIF ENCODE: median-cut palette + Floyd–Steinberg dithering, frame timing from the rational clock, loop control — the native --format gif path (no ffmpeg needed). Y4M/RAW: the uncompressed video containers (native no-ffmpeg video output; also the certified raw-frame stream's container option). WAV: PCM in/out (the mixer's substrate and the certified audio artifact).

DETERMINISTIC PARALLEL PNG SEQUENCES: sequence export encodes frames in parallel with FIXED DEFLATE BLOCK BOUNDARIES (pigz-style, on W8CODEC's compressor API) — deterministic bytes on ANY thread count, so the canonical PNG form survives under certified (PG-5 covers encoded PNGs, not just raw frames). This is a load-bearing determinism property, not an optimization nicety.

SVG EXPORT: vmobject_to_svg — trivial by design, since paths are native quads (emit path data + styles); document fidelity limits (gradients per our model, no attempt at filter effects).

Acceptance: GIF golden outputs (palette + dither determinism: same frames ⇒ same bytes); y4m header/frame conformance vs spec; WAV round-trip incl. sample formats; the parallel-PNG determinism test at {1,4,16} threads (bit-identical sequence bytes) wired into PG-5's per-commit sweep; SVG export round-trip (export → our own SVG importer → structural equality within tolerance).

title: W8: The negotiated ffmpeg boundary v2 — FFMPEG_PROTOCOL.md, sandbox, hardware encoders, fake-ffmpeg CI
description:
Implement the ONE external-tool boundary (§14.3, §3 D2, D-23). FFMPEG_PROTOCOL.md v2 replaces any fixed pipe with NEGOTIATION: fmn-frame renders in output orientation (no vflip, ever) and applies transfer natively (no obligatory eq); the sink negotiates pixel format (RGBA8/BGRA8 for alpha/compat, NV12 for ordinary 8-bit video, P010 for 10-bit/HDR, canonical RGBA for certified raw/PNG), orientation, transfer, primaries, range, row stride, and encoder. The arithmetic is the argument: 4K RGBA8 is 33.2MB/frame vs NV12's 12.4MB — 2.67x less pipe payload at 60fps.

THE SECURITY PROTOCOL (D2, complete): argv-only invocation (no shell), per-job private temp dirs, timeouts + process-tree cancellation (via fmn-platform's process capability), output-size limits, environment allowlist + locale pinning, atomic result publication, executable path AND content hash into provenance. ffmpeg is OPTIONAL: absence yields a capability error NAMING the native alternative — never silent substitution.

HARDWARE ENCODERS enter here and only here (§14.3): hevc_videotoolbox/ProRes on macOS, h264_nvenc/hevc_nvenc/AV1 on NVIDIA — a documented standard-mode knob (ffmpeg products are excluded from certification by construction, so hardware encode changes nothing about the determinism story); fmn doctor reports what the installed ffmpeg offers; selection explicit or auto; encoder identity in provenance.

Protocol retentions: GIF mode, the two-stage audio mux (video first; then -c:v copy -c:a aac -map ...), --subdivide outputs, insert files, --prerun counting. ALL testable against a FAKE-FFMPEG in CI (a scripted binary asserting argv/stdin contract and emitting canned outputs) — the protocol is CI-verified without real encoders.

Acceptance: fake-ffmpeg contract suite (every negotiation dimension, both mux stages, failure modes: timeout, oversized output, nonzero exit); sandbox tests (env allowlist, tree-kill on cancel); capability-error tests with ffmpeg absent; provenance fingerprint test; real-ffmpeg smoke test behind an env flag.

title: W8: The ordered asynchronous emitter — sequence-numbered frames, preallocated rings, never a stall
description:
Implement Reel's orchestration core (§14.1, D-19): sequence-numbered frames from the pipeline (§17.4) and from frame-parallel pure segments (§9.5) publish IN FRAME-INDEX ORDER through a preallocated ring of frame slots — Lumen never stalls on a sink, and no sink ever observes an out-of-order or torn frame.

Mechanics: a bounded ring sized by the RAM-derived, config-visible in-flight budget (PG-6: each in-flight 4K RGBA frame ≈ 33MB — the budget is real memory); workers deposit completed frames by index; the emitter drains the contiguous prefix to sinks (file writers, the ffmpeg pipe, the Studio stream, the y4m/PNG-sequence writers); backpressure propagates to the pipeline when the ring fills (bounded, deadlock-free: the ring never blocks a frame that the drain is waiting on — slot reservation happens at frame dispatch); the hot path never allocates, resizes, or synchronously flushes a frame-sized buffer (§14.3's rule).

Ordering is a determinism obligation, not a UX nicety (§10.5 e): frame-index-ordered emission is one of the parallelism contract's six clauses — the frame-parallel equivalence tests (W4PURITY, PG-5) depend on this component being airtight. Multi-sink: a render may feed video + PNG sequence + preview simultaneously; each sink observes the same ordered stream; a slow sink applies backpressure rather than dropping (drops are an explicit per-sink policy, only for preview-class sinks).

Acceptance: out-of-order deposit tests (adversarial completion orders ⇒ in-order emission, always); backpressure test (slow sink throttles pipeline without deadlock); torn-frame impossibility test (a slot is never visible to sinks mid-write — API review + concurrency test under the thread sweep); multi-sink consistency test; ring-budget accounting test vs the configured in-flight limit.

title: W8: fmn-cache — the content-addressed store
description:
Implement the persistent cache (§14.4): content-addressed on fmn-hash — atomic writes (write-temp + rename, never partial visibility), cross-process locks (two fmn invocations share a cache safely), per-entry checksums verified on read, versioned namespaces (a schema/version bump invalidates cleanly without clearing unrelated namespaces), corruption recovery (a bad entry is evicted and recomputed, never trusted, never fatal), traversal protection (keys never map to paths outside the cache root), defined eviction (LRU-class with pinning for in-use entries), size ceilings (config-visible).

Serves three consumers with one discipline: TYPESET RESULTS (fmd-math + text layout, keyed on string + preamble-pack hash + font hashes + engine version — W6TEX's contract; the dominant win, PG-7's <100µs cached path); FETCHED ASSETS (through the AssetFetcher capability — cached by content hash with the fetch policy recorded); THE REPLAY JOURNAL's storage (§13.4 — journal segments and checkpoint snapshots live under a cache namespace with pinning while a session is live).

Determinism interaction: a cache is an OPTIMIZATION, never an oracle — certified renders must produce identical output with a cold or warm cache (the key includes everything semantic, so a hit is definitionally equivalent; test it), and --clear-cache (the Reference flag, kept) must be safe at any moment.

Acceptance: atomicity tests (kill -9 mid-write leaves a consistent store); cross-process lock test (two writers, no corruption); corruption-injection tests (flipped bytes detected, evicted, recomputed); eviction/ceiling tests with pinning; cold-vs-warm equivalence test on a typeset-heavy scene; traversal fuzz on keys.

title: W8: The sound mixer — WAV/PCM with a fully specified matrix
description:
Implement audio (§14.5; displaces pydub): the WAV/PCM mixer with EVERY behavior specified — sample formats (i16/i24/f32) and rates with a NAMED resampler (documented algorithm and quality), channel layouts (mono/stereo mapping rules), gain semantics (gain, gain_to_background — the Reference's ducking parameter), negative time offsets (sound starting before its add point clips correctly), overlay-past-end (the mix extends), clipping policy (defined: clamp with a warning counter, not wraparound), dithering policy on bit-depth reduction, DETERMINISTIC MIX ORDER (sounds mix in a defined order so float summation is reproducible — certified WAV output is bit-locked), and A/V sync on the rational clock (sample-accurate placement from frame times — no float drift between audio and video, BN-02's audio corollary).

Scope boundaries: WAV in/out is native (W8CODEC2); NON-WAV inputs (mp3, etc.) decode through the ffmpeg capability boundary with named capability errors when absent (D2); certified output is WAV (ffmpeg's encoded audio is uncertified like all its products).

Scene surface: Scene.add_sound with the Reference's signature and semantics; the two-stage mux (W8FFMPEG) consumes the mixed WAV.

Acceptance: mix golden vectors (offsets, overlaps, gains, negative offsets, past-end) bit-locked; resampler quality tests (documented passband/stopband vs spec); clipping/dither policy tests; determinism test (same inputs ⇒ same WAV bytes, any thread count); A/V sync test (a click on frame N lands at the sample-exact time); ffmpeg-decode capability path test with the fake-ffmpeg.

title: W9: The Scene runtime — the state machine on the rational clock
description:
Implement Scene (§13.1, §1.4): the full runtime surface — construct; add/remove/bring_to_front/bring_to_back/clear with W3ORDER's stable-order semantics; play/wait/wait_until on the rational clock through W4's engine (the six-step order and FramePacket are Choreo's; the orchestration — begin/progress/finish per play, pre_play/post_play hooks — is here); SceneState snapshot/restore at the Reference's scope (time, play count, top-level mobjects + copies — via W3SNAP); EndScene; ThreeDScene defaults (camera orientation, ambient rotation hooks); presenter mode / hold loops; show(); BlankScene; the windowed fps=30 override (§1.4's quirk, kept as config semantics).

Also here: the typesetting PREFLIGHT hook (W6TEX's pre-play walk — the scene runtime exposes the constructed-mobject walk before first play); skip-mode (-s) and range flags (-n) semantics — skip renders nothing but advances state EXACTLY (updaters and RNG advance identically so post-skip state matches played state — a determinism property worth its own tests); frame-capture wiring to Reel's emitter.

The runtime is the integration point where Choreo/Marionette/Lumen/Reel meet — most of this bead's value is the CORPUS: end-to-end scene tests (our own primitive corpus, ~25 public scenes per G1) exercising the full loop with self-golden frame hashes.

Acceptance: scene lifecycle tests (construct/play/wait/state round-trips); skip-equivalence test (skip-to-end state ≡ play-to-end state, bit-for-bit on the arena snapshot); SceneState restore tests; ThreeDScene default fixtures; the primitive corpus running end-to-end with self-goldens locked.

title: W9: Events + InteractiveScene — the dispatcher and the interaction surface
description:
Implement interactivity (§13.2, §1.4): the event dispatcher (EventDispatcher/EventListener/EventType — registration, propagation, listener ordering per the Reference) and InteractiveScene's behaviors: selection (click/drag-rect selection of mobjects, selection highlight rectangles), grab/move, resize, color-picking, clipboard copy/paste of mobjects, keyboard-map COMPATIBLE with the Reference's bindings (the muscle-memory surface: users' hands know these keys).

Consumers: the Studio (W9STUDIO) forwards browser/TUI input events into this dispatcher; the interactive control mobjects (W7DETEX's Button/Checkbox/sliders/ControlPanel) register listeners here; MotionMobject/Textbox behaviors.

Design care: events interleave with the frame loop at a DEFINED point (they run in the serial front-end, before capture — they are scene-state mutations and pipeline barriers when they must observe pixels per §13.4); event handling in a paused/presenter-hold state has defined semantics; the dispatcher is deterministic under replay (the journal records event streams with timestamps on the rational clock — replaying a session re-fires events identically, §13.4's contract).

Acceptance: dispatcher unit tests (registration/removal/ordering/propagation); InteractiveScene behavior tests against scripted event streams (select → drag → resize → color → paste, asserting scene-state outcomes); keyboard-map fixture vs the Reference's bindings; replay determinism test (recorded event stream re-applied ⇒ identical final state); frame-loop interleaving test (events never mutate mid-capture).

title: W9: Supervisor + scene-worker iteration — rebuild, restart, restore, replay (no dlopen)
description:
Implement live iteration (§13.3, D-14): the supervisor + scene-worker-subprocess architecture — code change detected → incremental rebuild → worker restart → restore nearest serialized checkpoint → replay journal from the last valid barrier. Crashes isolate to the worker (a panic in scene code never takes down the Studio or supervisor); the supervisor owns the UI connection, the build pipeline, and checkpoint storage; the worker owns scene execution and rendering. No dlopen, no hot-patching — process isolation is the reliability story AND the security story (untrusted content renders only in the isolated worker, §13.5).

Protocol: a versioned supervisor↔worker IPC (fmn-hash canonical serialization) carrying: scene enumeration, play/seek/scrub commands, frame streams (shared-memory or pipe per platform via fmn-platform), checkpoint push/restore, journal segments, crash reports (a worker crash yields a structured report + the journal tail — every crash is a replayable bug per §18's repro-bundle story).

Edit-to-frame is PG-4's second budget (<1s on a trailing edit of a 30s pure scene): the mechanism is checkpoint restore + journal replay (only the edited tail re-executes) + warm caches (typeset/compiled paths survive restart via fmn-cache). fmn-python preserves the literal IPython embed()/checkpoint_paste() workflow (W10's bridge exposes the hooks; the checkpoint machinery is here).

Acceptance: crash-isolation tests (worker panic → supervisor alive → auto-restart → checkpoint restore); rebuild-restart-restore cycle test with a scripted edit (measured against the PG-4 budget on the pinned profile); IPC protocol golden tests + version-skew rejection; checkpoint round-trip fidelity (restored state bit-equals pre-restart snapshot); an end-to-end autoreload session test.

title: W9: The replay journal + effect model — record everything, replay cheaply, invalidate conservatively
description:
Implement replay (§13.4, R16): the journal records COMMANDS AND EFFECTS — play/wait/add/remove operations, RNG substream states at barriers, camera/audio state, content hashes of every file/font/asset read, callback version hashes, ffmpeg invocations — with OPAQUE BARRIERS for non-replayable operations and CONSERVATIVE invalidation (when in doubt, a barrier).

Three consumers, one record: (1) the supervisor's edit-replay (W9SUPER: restore checkpoint, replay journal from the last valid barrier — entries whose recorded hashes still match replay from cache; a changed callback hash invalidates from that point); (2) the PURITY CLASSIFIER's evidence (§9.5: the effect model IS the classifier's vocabulary — segments whose recorded effects are pure are frame-parallel eligible; classifications are journaled per segment, R20); (3) the PIPELINE's synchronization vocabulary (§17.4: any operation that must observe rendered pixels is a pipeline barrier that drains in-flight frames; ordinary manim scene code never hits one — assert this on the corpus).

Soundness doctrine (R16): replay-cache unsoundness is the failure mode — the design answer is conservative invalidation everywhere: unrecognized effects are opaque; divergent operation classes become barriers BY DEFAULT; a replay that would diverge (hash mismatch mid-replay) falls back to full re-execution, correctly, silently, and records why.

Also here: the one-command REPRO BUNDLE (§18) — scene + input closure packaged so every bug report is a deterministic replay — and the journal's appearance in provenance sidecars.

Acceptance: record/replay equivalence tests (replayed session ⇒ bit-identical final state + frames on pure content); invalidation tests (edited callback → replay stops at the right barrier); opaque-barrier tests (an unrecognized effect forces re-execution, never wrong reuse); purity-evidence round-trip with W4PURITY; pipeline-barrier drain test; a repro-bundle end-to-end test (bundle → fresh machine sim → identical render).

title: W9: The Studio — preview server, inspector, scrubbing, TUI, security model
description:
Implement the Studio (§13.5, D-14, leapfrog #6): localhost with an EXPLICIT security model — loopback-only binding, per-request capability token, Host/Origin validation, size limits, no filesystem serving beyond embedded UI assets, event-rate limits, session expiry. Untrusted content renders only in the isolated worker (W9SUPER).

Streams: baseline is MULTIPART PNG (we own PNG — no dependency, works everywhere); MJPEG as the ffmpeg-accelerated option; browser-local WASM rendering is the endgame (G5). On supported Apple silicon the preview renders through the METAL ANNEX by default (W5METAL — speed welcome, bit promises irrelevant for preview) with the CPU fallback declared (OQ-12/G3's rule).

Surfaces: TIMELINE SCRUBBING over W4TIMELINE + checkpoints (seek anywhere; pure segments reconstruct instantly, stateful segments snap to checkpoints); THE INSPECTOR — family tree, live record fields (the RecordBuffer's typed views), uniforms, TYPESET SPAN MAPS (W6SPAN's in-Studio payoff: hover a glyph, see its source span); DEBUG OVERLAYS — tiles, control cages, bounds, winding, depth (§18); a kitty/sixel TUI in the fp-frankentui lineage for SSH sessions (leapfrog #5's terminal leg).

Embedded UI: assets compiled into the binary (no runtime file serving, no CDN — the one-binary story extends to the Studio).

Acceptance: security tests (non-loopback refused; missing/stale token refused; oversized/flooding events limited; no path escapes); stream conformance (multipart PNG consumable by stock browsers); scrub correctness tests (seek(t) state matches play-through, via W4TIMELINE's machinery); inspector data-accuracy tests against engine state; TUI smoke test over a pty; annex-preview fallback test.

title: W9: The fmn CLI + batch + doctor — the Reference's flags where they still mean something
description:
